column6: Positive Electrode Active Material, Method for Manufacturing Positive Electrode Active Material, and Secondary Battery
column7: Provided is a positive electrode active material for a lithium ion secondary battery having favorable cycle characteristics and high capacity. A covering layer containing aluminum and a covering layer containing magnesium are provided on a superficial portion of the positive electrode active material. The covering layer containing magnesium exists in a region closer to a particle surface than the covering layer containing aluminum is. The covering layer containing aluminum can be formed by a sol-gel method using an aluminum alkoxide. The covering layer containing magnesium can be formed as follows: magnesium and fluorine are mixed as a starting material and then subjected to heating after the sol-gel step, so that magnesium is segregated.

column6: PARALLEL CHARGING AND POWER SUPPLY SYSTEM FOR PURE ELECTRIC VEHICLE
column7: A parallel charging and power supply system for a battery electric vehicle comprises a battery unit, a battery management system, a vehicle control unit, a motor controller and a motor, wherein a corresponding signal output end and a signal input end of the battery management system are respectively connected with a corresponding signal input end and a signal output end of the battery unit; the motor controller is respectively connected with a power output end of the battery unit, a power input end of the motor and a signal output end of the motor; a signal input end of the vehicle control unit is respectively connected with the corresponding signal output ends of the battery management system and the motor controller; the battery unit itself is provided with a conversion module for controlling the charging of the battery unit itself according to the commands of the battery management system, and a signal input end of the conversion module is connected with the corresponding signal output end of the battery management system; the power input end of the motor is connected with the power output end of the motor controller for obtaining electric energy and outputting the mechanical energy to drive the vehicle to travel; and the signal output end of the motor is connected with the corresponding signal input end of the motor controller. The parallel charging and power supply system for the battery electric vehicle of the present disclosure not only greatly increases the traveling mileage of the battery electric vehicle, but also possesses high safety, light weight, low price, and simple and quick charging.

column6: Positive electrode for nonaqueous secondary battery, method for forming the same, nonaqueous secondary battery, and electrical device
column7: A positive electrode for a nonaqueous secondary battery including an active material layer which has sufficient electron conductivity with a low ratio of a conductive additive is provided. A positive electrode for a nonaqueous secondary battery including an active material layer which is highly filled with an active material, id est, including the active material and a low ratio of a conductive additive. The active material layer includes a plurality of particles of an active material with a layered rock salt structure, graphene that is in surface contact with the plurality of particles of the active material, and a binder.

column6: Method for optimizing power distribution of fuel cell vehicle
column7: A rational fuel-cell power following strategy is made according to values such as vehicle fuel-cell power, battery power, and SOC (state of charge) of a lithium-ion battery; in the same time window, effects of different fuel-cell power growth rates on SOC of the lithium-ion battery are tested according to vehicle requirements; and at the same fuel-cell growth rate, effects of different time windows on SOC of the lithium-ion battery are tested according to vehicle requirements; a proper time window and a proper fuel-cell power change rate are found, so that the SOC value of the lithium-ion battery fluctuates within a certain range. The present invention can achieve a good operation mode of power distribution between the fuel cell and the lithium-ion battery, ensuring rational utilization of resources, thereby extending the application range of the lithium-ion battery to the maximum extent.

column6: INSTALLATION STRUCTURE FOR LITHIUM-ION BATTERY BOX OF ELECTRIC VEHICLE
column7: The present invention relates to an installation structure for a lithium-ion battery box of an electric vehicle, comprising a battery box body and a frame oblique beam, wherein a jack is made in the front end of the battery box body; an installation groove is made in the frame oblique beam; a front battery box base is installed in the front installation groove; a bolt driven through a key is arranged on the front battery box base; and locking fixation between the battery box body and the frame oblique beam is realized by inserting the bolt into the jack. The installation structure for the lithium-ion battery box of the electric vehicle is characterized in that: an upwards-bulged arc-shaped limit groove is made in the axis of the middle part of the lower cover of the box body; the installation groove of the frame oblique beam is an installation groove with a horizontal opening; a limit bulge adaptive to the arc-shaped limit groove is made on the groove bottom of the installation groove; and installation for the battery box body and a frame sectional material is realized through embedding for the arc-shaped limit groove and the limit bulge. The present invention has the advantages of being scientific and reasonable in structural design, simple in structure, convenient in disassembly and assembly, beneficial to frame design, high in aesthetics, and capable of meeting the use needs of users.

column6: Lithium Metal Complex Oxide
column7: Provided is a novel lithium metal complex oxide powder, which is capable of maintaining stability of the slurry viscosity during storage of a slurry even when D50 thereof is less than 10 μm, and which is also capable of suppressing a decrease in the discharge capacity during high temperature cycles. Proposed is a lithium metal complex oxide which is characterized by having a D50 of less than 10 μm and a carbon amount per unit BET specific surface area of from 1100 ppm/(m2/g) to 7500 ppm/(m2/g).

column6: METHOD FOR OPTIMIZING POWER DISTRIBUTION OF FUEL CELL VEHICLE
column7: A rational fuel-cell power following strategy is made according to values such as vehicle fuel-cell power, battery power, and SOC (state of charge) of a lithium-ion battery; in the same time window, effects of different fuel-cell power growth rates on SOC of the lithium-ion battery are tested according to vehicle requirements; and at the same fuel-cell growth rate, effects of different time windows on SOC of the lithium-ion battery are tested according to vehicle requirements; a proper time window and a proper fuel-cell power change rate are found, so that the SOC value of the lithium-ion battery fluctuates within a certain range. The present invention can achieve a good operation mode of power distribution between the fuel cell and the lithium-ion battery, ensuring rational utilization of resources, thereby extending the application range of the lithium-ion battery to the maximum extent.

column6: Vehicle
column7: A vehicle has a motor and an engine each serving as a driving source for running the vehicle, and assembled batteries each capable of supplying an electric power to the motor. The assembled batteries include a high-power assembled battery and a high-capacity assembled battery. The high-power assembled battery is capable of charge and discharge with a current relatively larger than that in the high-capacity assembled battery, and the high-capacity assembled battery has an energy capacity relatively larger than that of the high-power assembled battery. In running of the vehicle using an output from the motor with the engine stopped, the high-capacity assembled battery supplies a more electric power to the motor than that in the high-power assembled battery. The high-power assembled battery is placed in a riding space where a passenger rides, and the high-capacity assembled battery is placed in a luggage space different from the riding space.
column8: KIMURA KENJI;;SATO AKIHIRO;;TOYOTA MOTOR CO LTD

column6: Lithium ion batteries with long cycling performance
column7: Batteries with high energy and high capacity are described that have a long cycle life upon cycling at a moderate discharge rate. Specifically, the batteries may have a room temperature fifth cycle discharge specific energy of at least about 175 Wh/kg discharged at a C/3 discharge rate from 4.2V to 2.5V. Additionally, the batteries can maintain at least about 70% discharge capacity at 1000 cycles relative to the fifth cycle, with the battery being discharged from 4.2V to 2.5V at a C/2 rate from the fifth cycle through the 1000th cycle. In some embodiment, the positive electrode of the battery comprises a lithium intercalation composition with optional metal fluoride coating. Stabilizing additive maybe added to the electrolyte of the battery to further improve the battery performance. The batteries are particularly suitable for use in electric vehicles.

column6: Lithium ion constant electric battery control system and lithium ion constant electric battery
column7: The invention discloses a lithium ion constant electric battery control system which comprises a lithium ion constant electric battery single body control board, a two-way DC/DC converter and a charging and discharging port. One end of the two-way DC/DC converter is connected with a vehicle-mounted DC/DC power source through the charging and discharging port, the other end of the two-way DC/DC converter is connected with the lithium ion constant electric battery single body control board, and the lithium ion constant electric battery single body control board and a lithium ion constant electric battery module are connected; the two-way DC/DC converter is used for controlling the vehicle-mounted DC/DC power source to charge the lithium ion constant electric battery module when detecting input current from the vehicle-mounted DC/DC power source or controlling the lithium ion constant electric battery module to discharge electricity when not detecting the input current from the vehicle-mounted DC/DC power source. In addition, the invention discloses a lithium ion constant electric battery. By the adoption of the lithium ion constant electric battery control system and the lithium ionconstant electric battery, energy is saved, and the service life of the constant electric battery is prolonged.

column6: Dual Lithium-Ion Battery System for Electric Vehicles
column7: A battery system for powering a vehicle is provided. The system may include a first lithium-ion battery pack having a first total energy capacity and a first power to energy ratio (P/E ratio) and a second lithium-ion battery pack connected in parallel with the first lithium-ion battery pack and having a second total energy capacity that is higher than the first total energy capacity and a second P/E ratio that is lower than the first P/E ratio. A method of controlling the battery system is also provided, and may include controlling an operation of a vehicle according to a total power capability of the first and second battery strings, wherein the total power capability is the sum of a first battery string power capability and a second battery string power capability at a same voltage.
column9: YANG XIAO GUANG;;ARSENAULT RENATA MICHAELA;;ANDERSON RICHARD DYCHE

column6: Lithium ion battery life abnormity detection method
column7: The invention provides a lithium ion battery life anomaly detection method, which comprises the following steps of: calibrating coulombic efficiency of a target lithium ion battery to obtain two coulombic efficiency curves, and further obtaining a modified equation of a first fitting curve and a modified equation of a second fitting curve; secondly, for an electric vehicle loaded with a lithium ion battery with the same model as the target lithium ion battery, the coulombic efficiency ys and the number of circulation turns of the lithium ion battery of the electric vehicle are obtained, a standard coulombic efficiency upper limit value ymax and a standard coulombic efficiency lower limit value ymin are obtained based on the number of circulation turns of the lithium ion battery of the electric vehicle, and if ys is larger than ymax or ys is smaller than ymin, the coulombic efficiency of the lithium ion battery of the electric vehicle is judged to be larger than ymax or smaller than ymin. The electric vehicle lithium ion battery life detection method has the advantages of being low in cost and short in consumed time.

column6: LITHIUM-ION BATTERY AND APPARATUS CONTAINING SAME
column7: A lithium-ion battery and an apparatus containing the same are provided. In some embodiments, the lithium-ion battery includes: a positive electrode plate including a positive electrode current collector and a positive electrode active substance layer; and an electrolyte including a non-aqueous organic solvent. A low-swelling adhesive layer and an oily adhesive layer are sequentially arranged between the positive electrode current collector and the positive electrode active substance layer; the low-swelling adhesive layer includes a low-swelling binder, and the oily adhesive layer includes a first binder, where a solubility parameter SP1 of the low-swelling binder is less than a solubility parameter SP2 of the first binder.

column6: ULTRA-FAST CHARGING METHOD FOR FORKLIFT BATTERY
column7: Electric powered forklifts or cleaning machine have attracted much attention in the last decade because of their very substantial benefits. The main issue facing the efficiency of Electric forklifts is the charging time needed for the battery pack. To achieve the Ultra-fast charging for the battery. An AC-DC or DC-DC converter is added inside the battery compartment. The converter enables the charger to supply high power and voltage as the converter step-down the voltage to the standard battery voltage. By using this method, the charging time will be reduced without using big or bulky cables.

column6: SYSTEM FOR HEATING A LITHIUM-ION BATTERY USING ALTERNATING CURRENT AND A VEHICLE HAVING THE SAME
column7: A vehicle having an ambient temperature sensor, a low voltage lithium-ion battery and a controller is provided. The controller is configured to monitor the ambient temperature sensor and based on a determination that an ambient temperature is below a threshold value, provide an alternating current power to the low voltage lithium-ion battery. The alternating current power causes the low voltage lithium-ion battery to heat.

column7: The invention relates to the technical field of automobile power sources, in particular to a battery-electric-vehicle power-source system with a lithium battery trailer. column6: PRE-LITHIATION OF BATTERY ELECTRODE MATERIAL
column7: A method for making a pre-lithiated electrode for a lithium ion battery cell, a method for making a battery with a pre-lithiated electrode, and an electric vehicle with a pre-lithiated electrode are provided. An exemplary method for making a pre-lithiated electrode for a lithium ion battery cell includes electrochemically connecting a magnesium-lithium alloy to the electrode. Further, the method includes pre-lithiating the electrode by transferring lithium ions from the magnesium-lithium alloy to the electrode. Also, the method includes electrochemically disconnecting the magnesium-lithium alloy from the electrode.

column6: LITHIUM-ION SECONDARY BATTERY AND RELATED PREPARATION METHOD THEREFOR, BATTERY MODULE, BATTERY PACK, AND DEVICE
column7: A lithium-ion secondary battery (5) and related preparation method thereof, battery module (4), battery pack (1) and apparatus. Specifically, a lithium-ion secondary battery (5) includes a positive electrode plate, a negative electrode plate, an electrolyte and a separator, wherein the positive electrode plate includes a positive electrode current collector and a first positive electrode active material layer and a second positive electrode active material layer sequentially disposed on at least one side of the positive electrode current collector; the lithium-ion secondary battery (5) satisfies: -1 ≤ log<sub>10</sub>(u/v)×w ≤ 15.5, wherein, u is a thickness of the first positive electrode active material layer in microns, v is a thickness of the second positive electrode active material layer in microns, w is a conductivity of the electrolyte at a temperature of 25°C in mS·cm<sup>-1</sup>. The lithium-ion secondary battery (5) has excellent performance such as low discharge resistance at low SOC and low gas production at high temperature.

column6: Intelligent power management system based on electric two-wheeled vehicle
column7: The invention relates to an intelligent power management system based on an electric two-wheeled vehicle. The system comprises a lithium battery pack which comprises a plurality of single lithium batteries connected in series and is used for providing energy for the electric two-wheeled vehicle during riding, a standby battery which is respectively connected with the plurality of single lithium batteries and is used for carrying out emergency charging when the electric two-wheeled vehicle is excessively discharged, a lithium ion detection unit which is used for detecting the lithium ion content of each single lithium battery in the lithium battery pack in real time when the electric two-wheeled vehicle is not ridden so as to determine the lithium ion increment within preset time, a voltage detection unit which is used for detecting the voltage of the electric two-wheeled vehicle, a motor control unit which is used for controlling the riding of the electric two-wheeled vehicle, and a control unit which is used for controlling the standby battery to charge the lithium battery pack. Therefore, the speed of lithium battery damage caused by decomposition of lithium ion active substances due to excessive discharge can be slowed down, and the service life of the lithium battery of the electric two-wheeled vehicle is effectively prolonged.

column6: Lithium Transition Metal Oxide Having Layered Structure
column7: Provided is a lithium metal compound oxide having a layered structure, which is very excellent as a positive electrode active material of a battery that is mounted on, particularly, an electric vehicle or a hybrid vehicle. Suggested is a lithium metal compound oxide having a layered structure which is expressed by general formula of Li1+xM1−xO2 (M represents metal elements including three elements of Mn, Co, and Ni). In the lithium metal compound oxide having a layered structure, D50 is more than 4 μm and less than 20 μm, a ratio of a primary particle area to a secondary particle area of secondary particles having a size corresponding to the D50 (“primary particle area/secondary particle area”) is 0.004 to 0.035, and the minimum value of powder crushing strength that is obtained by crushing a powder using a microcompression tester is more than 70 MPa.

column6: Lithium ion battery remaining capacity early warning diagnosis test method for electric vehicle
column7: The invention discloses a lithium ion battery remaining capacity early warning diagnosis test method for an electric vehicle. The method comprises the following steps: S1, collecting the voltage U andthe power consumption Q of a lithium ion battery for the electric vehicle in the running process, collecting the environment temperature TE and the temperature TLi of the lithium ion battery at the same time, and determining a road surface influence coefficient psi according to a running road surface; S2, obtaining a voltage performance index xiU of the lithium ion battery and a voltage relativechange rate E of the lithium ion battery; and S3, obtaining a lithium ion battery residual electric quantity early warning probability P according to the data in the S2, and carrying out early warningprompt on the lithium ion battery residual electric quantity. According to the invention, the test data of the lithium ion battery is comprehensively collected, and the voltage performance index of the lithium ion battery and the voltage relative change rate of the lithium ion battery are obtained according to the test data. A residual electric quantity early warning diagnosis test model based ona fuzzy control model is established, and the residual electric quantity of the lithium ion battery for the electric vehicle can be early warned and prompted.

column6: Method for analyzing burn-in path dependence of lithium-ion battery
column7: The invention discloses a method for analyzing the burn-in path dependence of a lithium-ion battery. The method comprises the following steps: testing an obsolete lithium-ion battery used for a pure electric vehicle, analyzing the distribution characteristics of the capacity and the internal resistance of the lithium-ion battery, and determining a burn-in state of the obsolete lithium-ion battery used for the pure electric vehicle and the relevance between capacity and internal resistance changes; explaining a concept of lithium-ion battery burn-in path dependence based on the distribution characteristic analysis of the capacity and the internal resistance in conjunction with two possible generalized burn-in paths; building a cycle life experimental database of the lithium-ion battery for a specific actual running environment of the pure electric vehicle in order to quantitatively analyze the burn-in path dependence of the lithium ion battery in a laboratory; quantitatively analyzing the influences of a temperature, a discharging depth and thermal drift on a lithium-ion battery burn-in path and a corresponding degradation mechanism by applying an electrochemical in-situ analysis means based on the cycle life experimental database of the lithium-ion battery in order to form the method for analyzing the burn-in path dependence of the lithium-ion battery.

column6: COMPOSITION FOR FORMING ELECTRODE ACTIVE MATERIAL LAYER FOR LITHIUM ION SECONDARY BATTERIES

This composition for forming an electrode active material layer for lithium ion secondary batteries is capable of producing a battery with extended life. After discharging the battery from a state of charge (SOC) of 100% to an SOC of 90% at 25°C and 2.5 C, the discharging is paused for 10 minutes and an increase in voltage at pause is measured. The internal resistance is calculated according to the following formula (2): Internal resistance = (Increase in voltage at pause (V)/Current during discharge (A)) x Facing area between positive electrode and negative electrode (cm<sup>2</sup>) whereby uneven reaction distribution in the battery, which causes a rapid decrease of the capacity (secondary deterioration), can be assessed.

column6: ELECTROLYTE FOR LITHIUM-ION BATTERY, LITHIUM-ION BATTERY, BATTERY MODULE, BATTERY PACK, AND APPARATUS
column7: This application provides an electrolyte for lithium-ion battery, a lithium-ion battery, a battery module, a battery pack, and an apparatus. The electrolyte provided in this application includes a non-aqueous solvent, and a lithium salt and an additive that are dissolved in the non-aqueous solvent, and the additive includes a cyclosiloxane and a fluoroether. This application further provides a lithium-ion battery including the foregoing electrolyte. In the electrolyte provided in this application, the cyclosiloxane and the fluoroether are used together to synergistically react on a surface of the positive electrode, combining silicon and oxygen containing groups and fluorine and oxygen containing groups to form an interface film containing silicon, oxygen, and fluorine, which not only ensures density of the interface film, but also strengthens oxidation resistance and thermal stability of the interface film, greatly improving both storage performance and cycling performance of the lithium-ion battery under high temperature.

column6: Serial SOC testing for improved fast-charge algorithm
column7: An automatically generated and customized fast charging process results in reduced degradation in the battery cell. An algorithm for a particular battery cell profile is automatically generated and customized to minimize degradation due to fast charging for that particular batch. To generate the custom algorithm, battery cell information is retrieved for a profile of a battery, wherein each battery profile may have a particular manufacturer, model, type, electrode batch, and potentially other specific identification information. Each battery cell is charged from a particular SOC level and at a selected C-rate, and then discharged. During discharge, the battery cell is monitored for detection of lithium plating or other undesirable effects. A lookup table is automatically generated from the battery cell information, and can be provided to devices and/or battery management systems. The BMS then uses the lookup table to apply a charging process that is customized to the on-board battery.

column6: MOTOR VEHICLE
column7: There is provided a motor vehicle including a lithium ion capacitor; an electrical load configured to transmit electric power to and from at least the lithium ion capacitor; and a control device. The control device is programmed to control the lithium ion capacitor such as to be charged and discharged according to a discharging excess pattern that provides a higher discharge current value than a charging current value, when a degree of degradation of the lithium ion capacitor reaches or exceeds a predetermined level by charging and discharging the lithium ion capacitor according to a charging excess pattern that provides a higher charging current value than a discharge current value.

column6: LITHIUM-METAL FREE ANODE FOR ELECTRIC VEHICLE SOLID STATE BATTERIES
column7: Apparatuses, systems, and methods of storing electrical energy for electric vehicles are provided. A battery pack can be disposed in an electric vehicle to power the electric vehicle. A battery cell can be arranged in the battery pack. The battery cell can have a housing. The housing can define a cavity within the housing. The battery cell can have a solid electrolyte arranged within the cavity. The battery cell can have a cathode disposed within the cavity along a first side of the solid electrolyte. The battery cell can have an anode. The anode can have a carbon nanotube structure. The anode can be disposed within the cavity along the second side of the solid electrolyte and separated from the cathode by the solid electrolyte. The carbon nanotube structure can have pores. The pores can be deposited with electrolyte material to retain lithium material received via the solid electrolyte.

column6: Serial SOC Testing for Improved Fast-Charge Algorithm
column7: An automatically generated and customized fast charging process results in reduced degradation in the battery cell. An algorithm for a particular battery cell profile is automatically generated and customized to minimize degradation due to fast charging for that particular batch. To generate the custom algorithm, battery cell information is retrieved for a profile of a battery, wherein each battery profile may have a particular manufacturer, model, type, electrode batch, and potentially other specific identification information. Each battery cell is charged from a particular SOC level and at a selected C-rate, and then discharged. During discharge, the battery cell is monitored for detection of lithium plating or other undesirable effects. A lookup table is automatically generated from the battery cell information, and can be provided to devices and/or battery management systems. The BMS then uses the lookup table to apply a charging process that is customized to the on-board battery.

column6: Surface-mediated cell-powered vehicles and methods of operating same
column7: This invention provides a vehicle powered by a surface-mediated cell (SMC)-based power source, comprising a vehicle frame, at least a wheel supporting the frame or a propeller connected to the frame, a drive unit connected to the wheel or propeller, and a power source electrically connected to the drive unit, wherein the power source contains at least a surface-mediated cell. The vehicle can be a micro-EV (using the SMC for the stop-start function), HEV, plug-in HEV, all-electric vehicle, power-assisted bicycle, scooter, motorcycle, tricycle, automobile, wheelchair, fork lift, golf cart, specialty vehicle, bus, truck, train, rapid-transit vehicle, boat, or air vehicle. The ultra-high power density enables the SMC to provide pulsed power or increased current demands when the vehicle is accelerating or hill-climbing. The SMC also enables the power source to recuperate the braking energy when the vehicle decelerates, brakes, or simply moves down-hill.

column6: Method for determining overcharge boundary condition of ternary lithium ion power battery
column7: The invention provides a method for determining overcharge boundary condition of a ternary lithium ion power battery, which comprises the following steps: (1) pretreating a ternary lithium ion battery; (2) connecting a test device, and performing an overcharge test under an adiabatic condition; (3) and after the adiabatic overcharge test is finished, analyzing an overcharge test curve of the ternary lithium ion battery, and determining an overcharge boundary value of the lithium ion power battery. The invention can determine the overcharge boundary condition of the ternary lithium ion battery.According to the method, the overcharge boundary condition of the ternary lithium ion battery covering parameters such as voltage, temperature and electric quantity can be determined, data support can be provided for a control strategy of a battery management system, guarantee is provided for safety and reliability of the battery system, and reliable basis is provided for formulation of a whole vehicle charging strategy of the electric vehicle, so that whole vehicle use safety of the electric vehicle can be guaranteed, and electric vehicle safety accidents caused by overcharge are reduced.

column6: ELECTROLYTE FOR LITHIUM-ION BATTERY, LITHIUM-ION BATTERY, BATTERY MODULE, BATTERY PACK, AND DEVICE
column7: This application provides an electrolyte for lithium-ion battery, a lithium-ion battery, a battery module, a battery pack, and an apparatus. The electrolyte provided in this application includes a non-aqueous solvent, and a lithium salt and an additive that are dissolved in the non-aqueous solvent, and the additive includes a cyclosiloxane and a fluoroether. This application further provides a lithium-ion battery including the foregoing electrolyte. In the electrolyte provided in this application, the cyclosiloxane and the fluoroether are used together to synergistically react on a surface of the positive electrode, combining silicon and oxygen containing groups and fluorine and oxygen containing groups to form an interface film containing silicon, oxygen, and fluorine, which not only ensures density of the interface film, but also strengthens oxidation resistance and thermal stability of the interface film, greatly improving both storage performance and cycling performance of the lithium-ion battery under high temperature.

column6: High purity lithium and associated products and processes
column7: High purity lithium and associated products are provided. In a general embodiment, the present disclosure provides a lithium metal product in which the lithium metal is obtained using a selective lithium ion conducting layer. The selective lithium ion conducting layer includes an active metal ion conducting glass or glass ceramic that conducts only lithium ions. The present lithium metal products produced using a selective lithium ion conducting layer advantageously provide for improved lithium purity when compared to commercial lithium metal. column6: LIFE-EXTENDING RECHARGE CONTROL FOR COLD WEATHER LITHIUM-ION POWER SUPPLIES
column7: A lithium-ion battery assembly configured to provide electric power to a vehicle. The battery assembly includes a plurality of battery cells interconnected to provide a combined electrical potential between positive and negative terminals of the battery assembly, and a printed circuit board assembly (PCBA) disposed adjacent to a first array of battery cells of the plurality of battery cells. The PCBA includes a collector plate electrically coupled with the first array of battery cells, a temperature sensor configured to obtain temperature readings, a plurality of heaters configured to generate heat using electrical power, and an assembly processor. The assembly processor is configured to obtain readings from the temperature sensor, determine an estimated battery cell temperature, and initiate a heating program by delivering electrical power to the plurality of heaters from an electrical power source when the estimated battery cell temperature is below a threshold.

column6: Solid-state battery cell assembly, preparation method thereof, battery and application of solid-state battery cell assembly
column7: The invention discloses a solid-state battery cell assembly and a preparation method thereof, a battery, a power utilization device and an energy storage device. According to the solid-state battery cell assembly disclosed by the embodiment of the invention, the base membrane is also arranged between the positive electrode and the solid-state electrolyte layer and/or between the negative electrode and the solid-state electrolyte layer, and the electrolyte is adsorbed on the base membrane. The base membrane contained in the solid-state battery cell assembly provided by the embodiment of the invention can effectively reduce the direct contact of the contained electrolyte with the electrode and the solid-state electrolyte layer, can reduce the consumption of the electrolyte in the charging and discharging process, and also reduces the absorption of the solid-state electrolyte layer to the electrolyte; the electrolyte is kept to play a good and stable infiltration effect on a solid-solid interface between the electrode and the solid electrolyte layer, so that the impedance between the solid-solid interface is obviously reduced, and the electrochemical performance of the battery comprising the solid-state battery cell assembly disclosed by the embodiment of the invention is obviously improved.

column6: Solid-state battery with improved interface performance and preparation method thereof
column7: The invention relates to the field of lithium ion batteries, in particular to a solid-state battery with improved interface performance and a preparation method of the solid-state battery. The solid-state battery capable of improving the interface performance comprises a positive electrode, a negative electrode and a solid-state electrolyte, and the solid-state electrolyte is prepared from a gel polymer electrolyte through an in-situ polymerization method. The solid-state electrolyte is prepared by adopting an in-situ polymerization technology, so that the electrolyte can be fully fused with the surface of the pole piece, and the interfacial compatibility of the pole piece and the electrolyte is improved. The preparation method of the solid-state battery is similar to an existing lithium ion battery preparation process, the equipment cost can be reduced, and industrial production of the solid-state battery is facilitated.

A method comprises according to the nominal molar ratio of various elements in a garnet-type solid electrolyte chemical formula LiALaBMCNDZrEO12, subjecting a Li source, a La source, a Zr source, a M source, and an N source to wet ball milling and mixing; then providing 1 to 5% of excess Li source; drying and sieving the paste; and calcining uniformly mixed powder at high temperature under a certain condition to obtain a solid electrolyte material having a garnet-type cubic phase structure and containing a trace amount of La2Zr2O7 structure. According to the solid-state electrolyte membrane, the preparation method thereof and the solid-state battery provided by the invention, the double-layer solid-state electrolyte membrane is prepared by using a high-molecular polymer, a halide solid-state electrolyte and a sulfide solid-state electrolyte, so that the redox potential of a positive electrode and the redox potential of a negative electrode are simultaneously compatible, and the solid-state electrolyte membrane has good mechanical strength and is easy to produce on a large scale; and the method can be used for constructing long-life all-solid-state batteries.

column7: The invention relates to a pole piece with multiple layers of solid electrolyte, a preparation method of the pole piece, a solid-state battery and a preparation method of the solid-state battery. According to the invention, the solid-state battery pole piece contains multiple electrolyte layers such as the interface modification layer and the interface softening layer, so that the interface contact between the solid-state electrolyte and the electrode active material and between the solid-state electrolyte and the battery pole piece is increased, the interface compatibility is improved and the interface stress is slowly released through interface softening, and a diaphragm can be replaced to prepare a diaphragm-free solid-state battery; a stable solid-state battery interface is constructed, and the cycling stability of the solid-state battery is improved. A high-pressure atomization spraying method is used, the solid-state battery pole piece with multiple electrolyte layers can be prepared, the uniformity of a multi-layer structure is guaranteed, agglomeration of tiny solute particles in the slurry introduction process is prevented, the battery pole piece containing multiple layers of solid-state electrolytes is uniformly prepared, and meanwhile the solid-state battery pole piece containing multiple layers of solid-state electrolytes is prepared. The thickness of each solid electrolyte layer is accurately controlled, and the safety and the energy density of the solid-state battery are improved.

column6: Solid-state battery and preparation method and application thereof
column7: The invention relates to the technical field of lithium ion batteries, and discloses a solid-state battery and a preparation method and application thereof. The solid-state battery comprises stacked battery cells, and each battery cell comprises a positive electrode, a negative electrode and a composite electrolyte arranged between the positive electrode and the negative electrode; the composite electrolyte comprises a first electrolyte and second electrolytes arranged on the two sides of the first electrolyte, the first electrolyte is a solid electrolyte membrane, and the second electrolytes are obtained by curing electrolyte containing additives. According to the solid-state battery provided by the invention, the solid-state electrolyte and the liquid-state electrolyte containing the additive are organically combined, so that the interface impedance between the electrolyte and the positive electrode and between the electrolyte and the negative electrode can be remarkably enhanced, and the solid-state battery with excellent cycle stability can be obtained.

Assembling equipment for assembling solid-state battery by using solid-state electrolyte, and testing method for electric conductivity of solid-state electrolyte
column7: The invention discloses assembling equipment for assembling a solid-state battery by using a solid-state electrolyte, and a testing method for the electric conductivity of the solid-state electrolyte.The testing method comprises the following steps of step a, preparing solid-state electrolyte powder into solid-state electrolyte sheets; step b, assembling the solid-state electrolyte sheets into the solid-state battery by using the assembling equipment of the solid-state battery, and applying pressure to the solid-state battery for a certain time; c, carrying out alternating-current impedance testing on the solid-state battery at different temperatures, obtaining EIS curves of the solid-state electrolyte at different temperatures, and reading the impedance value of the solid-state electrolyte from the EIS curves; and step d, according to the impedance value of the solid-state electrolyte, through a formula which is as shown in the specification, calculating the electric conductivity ofthe solid-state electrolyte. According to the assembling equipment, the electrical conductivity of the solid-state electrolyte can be measured only by using the solid-state battery assembling equipment and a press, the obtained result is equivalent to that of a traditional gold spraying test method, however, the involved materials are all low in price, and the preparation process of the samples issimple.

column7: The invention provides an all-solid-state battery and a manufacturing method thereof. The all-solid-state battery provided by the invention comprises a positive electrode, a negative electrode and a solid electrolyte positioned between the positive electrode and the negative electrode. The negative electrode comprises a first negative electrode and a second negative electrode; the second negativeelectrode is positioned on a side surface of the first negative electrode; and the solid-state electrolyte comprises a first solid-state electrolyte and a second solid-state electrolyte, the first solid-state electrolyte is located between the positive electrode and the first negative electrode, the second solid-state electrolyte is located between the positive electrode and the second negative electrode, and roughness of the second solid-state electrolyte is greater than that of the first solid-state electrolyte. In the all-solid-state battery, the roughness of the first solid electrolyte corresponding to an electrode main body part is relatively small so that problems that the battery is easily short-circuited and an overall impedance of the battery is relatively large due to relativelylarge roughness of the solid electrolyte are reduced; and the roughness of the second solid electrolyte is relatively high so that the problems that the roughness of the solid electrolyte is too low and bonding power of the electrode and the solid electrolyte is not strong are avoided.

column6: Energy storage battery and energy storage system
column7: The utility model discloses an energy storage battery and an energy storage system, the energy storage battery comprises a plurality of battery cell modules which are stacked along the height direction of the battery cell modules, each battery cell module is provided with a first positioning hole, and two ends of a positioning pin are respectively matched in two first positioning holes of two adjacent battery cell modules. The battery cell module comprises a casing, a plurality of battery cells, a positive plug connector and a negative plug connector. The plurality of battery cells are arranged in the casing along the thickness direction of the battery cells and are connected in series, the positive plug connector and the negative plug connector are arranged on the casing, the battery cells positioned on the outer side in the plurality of battery cells are a first battery cell and a second battery cell, the positive electrode of the first battery cell is electrically connected with the positive plug connector, and the negative electrode of the second battery cell is connected with the negative plug connector. And the two ends of the connecting piece are respectively connected with the casings of the two adjacent battery cell modules. According to the energy storage battery disclosed by the utility model, the battery cell modules do not need to be distinguished in the installation process, so that the assembly efficiency of the energy storage battery is improved, and two adjacent battery cell modules are accurately aligned.
column8: SHENZHEN WENCHU INNOVATION TECH CO LTD

column6: Energy storage battery module, energy storage battery cluster and energy storage battery system
column7: The invention provides an energy storage battery module, an energy storage battery cluster and an energy storage battery system. The energy storage battery module comprises a plurality of single batteries and a slave control module. The single batteries are connected in series and/or in parallel to form a positive electrode end and a negative electrode end; the positive electrode end is connected with a contactor in series, and the contactor comprises a contact and a coil. The contactor is normally open; and the slave control module drives the coil to be electrified or not. Only when the energy storage battery module works normally and has no fault, the coil is electrified, the contactor is closed, and a voltage can be output outwards; when faults such as battery thermal runaway, loop overcurrent or overvoltage occur, the coil loses power, the contactor is switched off, and no voltage is output to the outside, so that whether the energy storage battery module outputs to the outside or not is controlled within the range of the energy storage battery module, the fault response and control protection capability can be improved, and the safety is improved.

column6: Battery energy storage system and method for battery energy storage system

The battery energy storage system includes at least one sensor for detecting response data of the battery in charging or discharging by superimposing a control signal. column6: Thermal runaway suppression method and energy storage battery system
column7: The invention provides a thermal runaway suppression method and an energy storage battery system that are applied to the technical field of direct current energy storage; the method is applied to theenergy storage battery system provided with a bleeder circuit, and after preset information representing the running state of the energy storage battery system is obtained, if the obtained preset information represents that the energy storage battery system is in a thermal runaway state, the energy storage battery system is disconnected from downstream equipment, and the bleeder circuit is controlled to release the electric energy stored in the energy storage battery system until a preset end condition is reached. According to the method, when the energy storage battery system is in a thermalrunaway state, the electric energy stored in the energy storage battery system is released through the discharge circuit, so that the heat released by the energy storage battery system is reduced, andthe energy storage battery system is promoted to recover to a normal working state; even if the energy storage battery system is on fire, as the bleeder circuit releases the energy of the energy storage battery system, the probability of secondary after-combustion during fire extinguishing in a gas mode can be effectively reduced, and the fire extinguishing success rate is improved.

column6: Configuration optimization method and equipment for BMS battery energy storage system and medium

Analyzing to obtain a region configuration value of a region where the battery energy storage system is located; analyzing the environment condition of the area where the battery energy storage system is located, and analyzing to obtain an environment configuration value of the area where the battery energy storage system is located; setting the configuration of the energy storage equipment in the area where the battery energy storage system is located by combining the area configuration value and the environment configuration value to obtain the configuration parameters of the energy storage equipment in the area where the battery energy storage system is located; the configuration condition of the energy storage equipment in the area where the battery energy storage system is located is optimized according to the configuration parameters, equipment optimization signals or spacing optimization signals are generated, the energy storage equipment in the area where the battery energy storage system is located is adjusted according to the signals, and accurate optimization of the configuration of the battery energy storage system is achieved in combination with multiple factors.

column6: High-efficiency charging and discharging energy storage control system
column7: The utility model provides a high-efficiency charging and discharging energy storage control system. The high-efficiency charging and discharging energy storage control system comprises a communication front-end processor, an electricity price monitoring module, an energy management system, an energy storage battery system, a battery management system and an energy storage bidirectional converter, the communication front-end processor, the energy storage battery system and the energy storage bidirectional converter are all connected with the energy management system; the energy management system is used for coordinating and distributing electric energy and determining the charging and discharging time period of the energy storage battery system; the energy storage battery system is provided with a charging and discharging detection module and a plurality of battery modules, each battery module comprises a plurality of energy storage batteries, and the energy storage batteries are connected with battery management units and are in one-to-one correspondence with the battery management units; the charging and discharging detection module is connected with the battery module and is used for detecting the voltage of the energy storage battery and the charging and discharging support number of the battery management unit; and the battery management system is connected with the communication front-end processor and the energy storage battery system. The charging and discharging time period of the energy storage battery can be reasonably controlled, the service life of the energy storage battery is prolonged, and the cost is reduced.

column6: Thermal management method and device of energy storage system, computer equipment and storage medium
column7: The invention relates to an energy storage system thermal management method and device, computer equipment and a storage medium. The method is applied to the energy storage system, the energy storage system comprises at least one energy storage battery, and the energy storage battery comprises a plurality of battery cells. The method comprises the following steps: acquiring the real-time temperature of each battery cell, and determining the maximum battery cell temperature according to each real-time temperature; obtaining the working state of each energy storage battery; when the working state of at least one energy storage battery is a running state, defining the energy storage battery in the running state as a target battery, and determining the minimum battery cell temperature of each battery cell in the target battery; and performing temperature control on the energy storage system according to the maximum battery cell temperature and the minimum battery cell temperature. According to the invention, modularization of thermal management of the energy storage battery can be realized in the energy storage system, so that the safety of stable operation of the energy storage system is improved.

column6: Energy management and control method and system for battery energy storage system
column7: The embodiment of the invention provides an energy management and control method and system for a battery energy storage system. The method comprises the steps of obtaining operation data of an energystorage battery and an energy storage converter in a plurality of energy storage systems, and determining the operation states of the energy storage battery and the energy storage converter, whereineach energy storage system comprises a preset conventional battery energy storage system and an echelon utilization energy storage system; determining the energy storage power of each energy storage system according to the operation data and the operation state of the energy storage battery and the energy storage converter and a pre-acquired actual capability factor of each energy storage system;and performing energy monitoring and protection control on each corresponding energy storage system by applying each energy storage power. According to the invention, operation control and energy management control for the energy storage systems can be realized, the battery energy storage system can be efficiently and accurately applied to various scenes, the service life of the battery can be effectively prolonged while the application safety of the battery energy storage system is ensured, and the good performance of the energy storage system can be effectively maintained.

column6: State detection method and system for battery energy storage system

column6: Method For Controlling Battery Energy Storage System And Battery Energy Storage System
column7: A method for controlling a battery energy storage system and a battery energy storage system including at least three battery energy storage units, a switching arrangement, and a control arrangement configured to select at least two battery energy storage units such that a sum of short circuit currents of the selected battery energy storage units is below a short circuit current limit, which is less than a sum of short circuit currents of the at least three battery energy storage units, and such that a sum of discharge or charge powers of the selected battery energy storage units equals to or exceeds a power limit, and such that states of charge of the selected battery energy storage units are within a determined range, and to control the switching arrangement to couple only the selected at least two battery energy storage units to a DC power connection.

column6: Systems and methods for series battery charging
column7: Systems and methods for charging and discharging a plurality of batteries are described herein. In some embodiments, a system includes a battery module, an energy storage system electrically coupled to the battery module, a power source, and a controller. The energy storage system is operable in a first operating state in which energy is transferred from the energy storage system to the battery module to charge the battery module, and a second operating state in which energy is transferred from the battery module to the energy storage system to discharge the battery module. The power source electrically coupled to the energy storage system and is configured to transfer energy from the power source to the energy storage system based on an amount of stored energy in the energy storage system. The controller is operably coupled to the battery module and is configured to monitor and control a charging state of the battery module.

column6: Optimal configuration system of multi-type battery energy storage system

According to the energy storage system, the energy storage system is optimized through the integration of the intelligent battery energy storage control system and the intelligent remote monitoring system, intelligent control over the battery energy storage process is effectively achieved through the intelligent battery energy storage control system, and meanwhile the intelligent remote monitoring system is used for controlling the energy storage process of the battery. The battery energy storage and energy storage detection conditions can be known through the background terminal, so that the functionality and practicability of the battery energy storage equipment are effectively enhanced by optimizing the design of the system.

column6: Battery cluster control method and device, equipment and storage medium

column6: Battery energy storage system control device, method, equipment and medium
column7: The invention provides a battery energy storage system control device, method, equipment and medium, and the device comprises an energy storage monitoring module which is used for monitoring the battery capacity of a battery energy storage system in real time, the battery energy storage system is formed by connecting a plurality of batteries in series, the battery capacity is composed of the battery sub-capacity of each battery, and the energy storage monitoring module is used for monitoring the battery capacity of the battery energy storage system; the battery sub-capacity is obtained through an analog acquisition chip configured in each battery; the energy storage control module is used for controlling each battery of the battery energy storage system by adopting a layered low-power-consumption control strategy if the battery capacity of the battery energy storage system is monitored to be lower than the preset capacitance, and controlling the working mode of the corresponding analog acquisition chip by generating a control instruction, so that the battery energy storage system is in a low-power-consumption state; when the battery energy storage system is in the low electric quantity, the layered low-power-consumption control strategy is adopted to control each battery to be in the low-power-consumption state, the battery energy storage system is prevented from being over-discharged, the service life of each battery is ensured, and the charging and discharging performance of each battery is affected.

column6: Method for automatically awakening energy storage system battery with low electric quantity based on optical storage micro-grid
column7: The invention discloses a method for automatically waking up a battery of an energy storage system based on an optical storage micro-grid at a low electric quantity, and relates to the technical field of new energy storage, the system comprises a photovoltaic module, optical storage integrated equipment and an energy storage battery system, the photovoltaic module is connected with the optical storage integrated equipment, the optical storage integrated equipment is connected with the energy storage battery system, and the energy storage battery system is connected with the optical storage micro-grid. The energy storage battery system comprises a battery management system and an energy storage battery, and the battery management system is connected with the energy storage battery. Double voltage energy storage systems of extended range electric vehicle
column7: The invention discloses double voltage energy storage systems of an extended range electric vehicle. A load system is connected with a capacitor energy storage system directly; a battery energy storage system is connected with the capacitor energy storage system and the load system through a diode; when the vehicle brakes, the diode reversely cuts off and accordingly the electric braking recycled energy is recycled into the capacitor energy storage system and the voltage of the capacitor energy storage system is higher than that of the battery energy storage system after the recycling of the braking energy is achieved; when the vehicle is driven, the voltage of the capacitor energy storage system is higher than that of the battery energy storage system, the diode works in the cut-off state, and the capacitor energy storage system provides the energy for a driving system and the diode is conducted when the voltage of the capacitor energy storage system is lower than that of the battery energy storage system and the battery energy storage system starts to provide the energy for the driving system; when the remaining power of a battery reaches a set value, a contactor is closed, the power of the capacitor energy storage system flows back to the battery energy storage system to charge the battery. The double voltage energy storage systems of the extended range electric vehicle are small in charging current and discharging current, high in efficiency and long in service life.

column6: LITHIUM BATTERY FABRICATION PROCESS USING MULTIPLE ATMOSPHERIC PLASMA NOZZLES
column7: A first atmospheric plasma producing nozzle is used to direct a gas-borne stream of plasma heated and activated particles of lithium battery electrode material for deposition on a surface of lithium cell member, such as a separator or current collector foil. A second atmospheric plasma producing nozzle is used to direct a gas-borne stream of plasma heated and activated metal particles at the same surface area being coated with the stream of electrode material particles. The two plasma streams are combined at the cell member surface to form a layer of electrically-conductive metal-bonded particles of electrode material. The use of multiple atmospheric plasma streams is useful in making thin, efficient, and lower cost electrode structures for lithium batteries.

column6: LITHIUM-ION BATTERY WITH WIRE-CONTAINING ELECTRODES
column7: The electrical performance and structural integrity of lithium battery electrodes, formed of particles of active electrode materials, are improved by mixing electrically conductive wires (metal wires, carbon fibers, and/or the like, including chemically-reduced metal oxide particles) with the particles of active electrode material. For example, copper wires may be intimately mixed with anode particles in porous anode layers which are resin-bonded to sides of a copper current collector foil. And aluminum wires may be mixed with cathode particles in porous cathode layers resin bonded to an aluminum current collector. The wires may be used to increase both the conductivity of electrons and lithium ions and the flexibility of the electrode layer when the electrodes are infiltrated with a solution of a lithium salt electrolyte. The workable thickness of each electrode layer can thus be increased and its performance enhanced to produce a lower cost and better forming battery.

column6: METHODS FOR FAST-CHARGING BATTERIES
column7: Methods for fast-charging battery packs having at least one lithium battery cell with an anode, a cathode, and a reference electrode (RE) comprise charging the battery in a first phase by maximizing charging current, subsequently charging the battery in a second phase by decreasing the charging current in response to an anode potential (AP) determined by a RE to maintain the AP at or above an AP threshold, and subsequently charging the battery in a third phase by decreasing the charging current in response to the cathode potential (CP) determined by the RE such that the CP is maximized without exceeding the cathode potential threshold. A controller can determine anode potential or cathode potential in real time using a cell potential signal and a cathode RE signal or an anode RE signal, respectively. The AP threshold is the AP above which substantially no lithium plating occurs.

column6: ELECTRODE DESIGNS FOR LITHIUM ION BATTERY AND CAPACITOR HYBRID SYSTEM

The respective electrodes are formed of porous layers of one of lithium anode material particles, lithium cathode material particles, or compatible capacitor material particles, formed on one or both sides of a compatible current collector foil. The amounts of active battery and capacitor particles are managed by the thickness of the porous coating layers, and one-side or two-side electrode coatings, to balance the capacities of the battery and capacitor particles to accept and release lithium ions during repeated charging and discharging of the hybrid cell.

column6: Water based hybrid lithium ion capacitor battery having a water-in-salt electrolyte

The water-in-salt electrolytic solution includes a sufficient amount of a lithium salt disposed in an aqueous solvent, at least 14 moles of lithium salt per kg of aqueous solvent, such that a dissociated lithium ion is solvated by less than 4 water molecules. The plurality of electrodes includes a first type electrode, a second type electrode, and a third type electrode selectively assembled in a predetermined order of arrangement into an electrode stack assembly. The first type electrode includes an activated carbon, the second type electrodes include one of a lithium manganese oxide (LMO) and titanium dioxide (TiO2), and the third type electrodes include the other of the LMO and TiO2. The first type electrode may be that of a cathode and/or anode.

column6: Methods for fast-charging batteries
column7: Methods for fast-charging battery packs having at least one lithium battery cell with an anode, a cathode, and a reference electrode (RE) comprise charging the battery in a first phase by maximizing charging current, subsequently charging the battery in a second phase by decreasing the charging current in response to an anode potential (AP) determined by a RE to maintain the AP at or above an AP threshold, and subsequently charging the battery in a third phase by decreasing the charging current in response to the cathode potential (CP) determined by the RE such that the CP is maximized without exceeding the cathode potential threshold. A controller can determine anode potential or cathode potential in real time using a cell potential signal and a cathode RE signal or an anode RE signal, respectively. The AP threshold is the AP above which substantially no lithium plating occurs.

column6: PRE-LITHIATION OF ANODES FOR HIGH PERFORMANCE CAPACITOR ASSISTED BATTERY
column7: A hybrid lithium-ion battery/capacitor cell comprising at least a pair of graphite anodes assembled with a lithium compound cathode and an activated carbon capacitor electrode can provide useful power performance properties and low temperature properties required for many power utilizing applications. The graphite anodes are formed of porous layers of graphite particles bonded to at least one side of current collector foils which face opposite sides of the activated carbon capacitor. The porous graphite particles are pre-lithiated to form a solid electrolyte interface on the anode particles before the anodes are assembled in the hybrid cell. The pre-lithiation step is conducted to circumvent the irreversible reactions in the formation of a solid electrolyte interface (SEI) and preserve the lithium content of the electrolyte and lithium cathode during formation cycling of the assembled hybrid cell. The pre-lithiation step is also applicable to other anode materials that benefit from such pre-lithiation.

column6: SAFETY MONITORING METHOD AND SYSTEM FOR VEHICLE, AND DEVICE
column7: The invention relates to the technical field of battery safety, and in particular to a safety monitoring method and system for a vehicle. The invention is intended to solve the problem that a battery cannot be continuously and safely monitored when a new energy vehicle is parked. To this end, the safety monitoring method in the invention includes: when a vehicle is in a parked state, waking up a battery management controller at specific time intervals, and obtaining status data of the battery by means of a battery management controller; determining, based on the status data, whether the battery is in an anomalous state; and when the battery is not in the anomalous state, controlling the battery management controller to enter hibernation state again; or when the battery is in the anomalous state, further waking up a wireless communications network, and uploading the status data and/or an alarm signal to a remote monitoring platform by means of the wireless communications network. According to the present application, a status of a traction battery in a parked state can be continuously monitored, and in addition, data can be remotely uploaded purposefully to reduce energy consumption.

column6: Battery event monitoring system for thermal managed battery
column7: The present invention provides a system and method for determining when a battery, or one or more batteries within a battery pack, undergoes an undesired thermal event such as thermal runaway. Cloud connectivity can be provided via a mobile application to a battery thermal management package.

column6: Bidirectional Adaptive Terminal Voltage (BATV) with a Battery Pack
column7: A Bidirectional Adaptive Terminal Voltage (BATV) system that enables a battery system to be integrated into various devices (or external loads), without having to modify the electrical characteristics of the devices. The battery system includes a battery cell stack and a battery management system electrically coupled with one another. The battery management system is further coupled with the BATV system, which is a bidirectional converter configured to operate in either a buck or boost mode, depending on the voltage conditions of an external load when power is required to be delivered from the battery system to the external load. When the battery system is being recharged from an external power supply or from regenerative energy absorption, the BATV system also operates in either buck or boost mode, as required by recharging conditions.

column6: BATTERY SAFETY SYSTEM AND METHOD

The system configures the first thermal regulation system in response to the comparison, and the second thermal regulation system is configured if any of the one or more parameters exceeds the corresponding threshold value.

column6: Battery pack configuration to reduce hazards associated with internal short circuits
column7: A battery pack, or battery pack module, is provided that is configured to respond to a short circuit of moderate current in a manner that minimizes the risk of an initial thermal runaway event propagating throughout the battery pack/battery pack module. In general, the battery pack/battery module allows pre-selection of which cell of the cells comprising the battery pack/battery pack module will be the last cell to respond to the short circuit. As a result, a thermal isolation barrier may be used to separate the preselected cell from the other cells of the battery pack/battery pack module, thereby minimizing the risk of excessive heating and extensive collateral damage.

column6: Tubular battery pack and integral tubular battery with thermal management and safety relief system

column6: Highly-integrated fail operational e-powertrain for autonomous driving application
column7: Embodiments of the present disclosure are directed to using Automotive Safety Integrity Level (ASIL) decomposition and a functional deployment strategy to redundantly distribute functionality across existing controllers. Accordingly, each of a plurality of non-redundant controllers of the vehicle can execute a plurality of functions. The controllers can comprise a vehicle controller and a controller for each of a plurality of different powertrain systems. The plurality of functions can comprise one or more functions for each of the plurality of powertrain systems and each of the functions can execute on multiple controllers. When a failure of one controller occurs, this can be detected either by a controller's build-in internal monitor or by a two-way comparison or three-way voting by external controllers. The functions executing on the failed controller can be transitioned to other controllers based on prior execution of those functions by the other controllers.

According to the method, the preset battery thermal management control parameter corresponding to the current battery state is corrected by acquiring the current environment temperature and the battery state of the power battery to obtain the corrected target control parameter, so when the battery thermal management control is carried out according to the target control parameter, battery thermal management control is carried out according to the corrected target control parameter; therefore, the charging and discharging requirements of the battery can be ensured, and the influence on the vehicle safety and the battery safety can be avoided.

The first subsystem is provided with a first electronic control unit for each of said modules, and the second subsystem is provided with a second electronic control unit, distinct from, and operatively coupled to, the first electronic control unit through a communication connection, so as to receive an alarm signal upon detection of the conditions indicative of the thermal runaway in the corresponding module.

column6: Passive safety management battery pack
column7: The invention relates to a passive safety management battery pack. The passive safety management battery pack comprises a plurality of battery cells and a liquid cooling device. Each battery cell hasa pole junction element. The liquid cooling device is arranged on the surface of the pole confluence element in a heat conduction and insulation manner. The liquid cooling device comprises a liquid cooling medium. The liquid cooling device is provided with a plurality of hot melting structures. The liquid cooling device comprises the liquid cooling medium and can cool a plurality of pole confluence elements to realize conventional cooling so as to realize a conventional heat management function. When thermal runaway happens to the single battery cells, released high-temperature flue gas and the like can melt the hot melting structures, the internal liquid cooling medium submerges the interior and the exterior of the battery from top to bottom, the thermal runaway battery is cooled in an evaporation heat exchange mode, a flowing heat transfer mode and the like, more efficient cooling is achieved, and then the function of emergency heat management is achieved. Therefore, through the passive safety management battery pack, the functions of conventional thermal management and emergency thermal management are achieved at the same time, and the two working modes of conventional thermal management and emergency thermal management are achieved.

column6: Method and system for monitoring thermal runaway after power-off dormancy and electronic equipment
column7: The invention relates to the technical field of vehicles, and discloses a thermal runaway monitoring method and system after power-off dormancy and electronic equipment. The method comprises the following steps: when the battery management system is in a dormant state, acquiring first battery state data of a battery pack; if it is determined that the first battery state data exceed a set battery data threshold value, the battery management system is awakened; acquiring second battery state data of the battery pack, and analyzing the second battery state data to obtain a safety assessment result of the battery pack; and determining the thermal runaway state of the battery pack according to the second battery state data and/or the safety evaluation result. Thus, when the vehicle is in the power-off dormant state, if the risk of thermal runaway exists, the battery management system can be awakened in time, the obtained battery state data are analyzed through the battery management system, accurate monitoring of the thermal runaway of the battery pack is achieved, and the safety of a vehicle owner and personnel nearby the vehicle is effectively ensured.

The battery management system performs safety diagnosis according to the voltage parameters of the battery packs and closes contactors in order. The battery management system selects the thermal management mode according to the temperature state of each battery pack and transmits a request signal to a finished automobile controller. column6: Battery thermal management method and device

The method comprises the following steps: judging whether a target representative value in at least one battery temperature representative value of a single battery in a battery pack exceeds a corresponding thermal management threshold value or not; if yes, controlling the battery thermal management system to work; collecting preset energy consumption parameters of the battery thermal management system to determine comprehensive energy consumption of the battery thermal management system, and judging whether the comprehensive energy consumption is within an energy consumption range allowed by the battery thermal management system or not; if yes, re-determining the target representative value based on the current temperature of the single battery in the battery pack, and judging whether the re-determined target representative value exceeds the corresponding safety threshold value or not; if not, adjusting the thermal management threshold value corresponding to the target representative value. According to the method and the device, the thermal management threshold value can be dynamically adjusted instead of a fixed thermal management threshold value, so that energy waste of the battery thermal management system can be avoided, and the energy consumption management level of the whole vehicle is improved.

column6: Thermal safety detection method for battery pack
column7: The invention relates to a thermal safety detection method of a battery pack. The thermal safety detection method comprises the following steps: performing a thermogravimetric performance test on a battery material of a battery pack sample to determine the thermal stability of the battery material; performing an adiabatic temperature rise test on the single batteries of the battery pack sample to determine battery thermal performance parameters of the single batteries; and verifying whether the temperature precision and the temperature protection value of the battery management system of the battery pack sample are reasonable based on the battery thermal performance parameters. According to the method, the thermogravimetric performance of a battery material and the adiabatic temperature rise of a single battery are tested so as to determine the thermal stability of the battery material and the battery thermal performance parameters of the single battery, and then whether the temperature precision and the temperature protection value of a battery management system of a battery pack sample are reasonable or not is verified based on the battery thermal performance parameters. According to the comprehensive thermal safety detection method comprising at least three different tests, thermal failure of the battery can be avoided, and the test risk and the test cost are greatly reduced.

According to the power battery thermal management system capable of passively processing thermal runaway, a refrigerant (supercritical carbon dioxide) is applied to a battery thermal runaway passive safety system, so that the concentration and the temperature of combustible gas can be effectively and quickly reduced to be within the range of combustion and explosion when a battery cell is subjected to thermal runaway, the safety of the battery is effectively improved, and the life and property safety of personnel is protected.

column6: Power battery and vehicle

According to the scheme provided by the utility model, when the battery module is subjected to thermal runaway, heat emitted by the battery module heats the thermal management flow channel, the thermal management flow channel is unsealed when being heated to reach the preset temperature threshold value, and the cooling liquid flows out of the thermal management flow channel to be in contact with the battery module so as to cool the battery module; therefore, the thermal runaway of the power battery is relieved and controlled, and the safety and the reliability are improved.
