column6: Battery safety system for detecting analytes
column7: A battery safety system includes a flow valve and a sensing device. The flow valve is disposed on a housing of the battery and includes a first valve that is embedded inside the housing and a second valve that intersects the housing. The first valve includes a cavity through which analytes released upon electrochemical reactions within the battery flow towards the second valve. The second valve extends through the housing to the outside, and defines an opening through which the released analytes exit the housing. The sensing device is disposed within the cavity of the first valve of the valve and is situated in a manner to be in fluidic contact with the released analytes as they flow from the inside of the battery to the outside. In some aspects, the battery safety system can detect a minute presence of one or more analytes. In some aspects, the sensor is in communication with a battery management system.

column6: Regenerated lithium-ion cathode materials having modified surfaces

At least two different lithium-ion conducting species are on the surface of the core material, and the at least two different lithium-ion conducting species are selected from AlF3, Li3PO4, and a lithium metal oxide. Another regenerated cathode active material for use in a lithium-ion battery comprises a core material comprising a transition metal oxide and lithium, the core material having a surface, and multiple lithium-ion conducting species on the surface, wherein the core material is a recycled cathode active material that has been re-lithiated.

column6: MIXED CATHODE UPCYCLING
column7: A method for recycling secondary battery charge materials includes a one-step molten-salt process to upcycle mixed Ni-lean polycrystalline NMC cathodes into Ni-rich single-crystal NMC cathodes. The method includes receiving a recycling stream of charge materials from end-of-lifetime batteries, adding additional charge materials based on an upcycled battery chemistry intended for the upgraded, recycled battery, and sintering the combined charge materials for generating a single crystal charge material corresponding to the upcycled battery chemistry using a molten salt direct recycling process.

column6: Recycling device for waste lithium batteries
column7: The utility model discloses a recycling device for waste lithium batteries, which belongs to the technical field of lithium battery recycling, solves the problem of poor recycling effect, and has the technical key points that the recycling device comprises a treatment box, the waste lithium batteries are placed in saline water in a saline water tank for discharging treatment, so that subsequent lithium battery crushing treatment is facilitated; the arranged crushing mechanism can crush the falling lithium batteries, a sieve plate can fall back and forth under the action of gravity, so that vibration is formed, blockage of the sieve plate is avoided, the arranged treatment mechanism can dry the crushed lithium batteries, the falling lithium batteries can be crushed again, metal can be adsorbed, and the service life of the lithium batteries is prolonged. The first filter screen can filter electrolyte in the lithium battery, the second liquid discharging pipe can discharge the filtered electrolyte, the second filter screen and the activated carbon plate arranged in the air outlet pipe can purify and sterilize exhausted gas, and the recycling device has the advantage of being good in recycling effect.
column8: SHANGHAI YONGHAN MATERIAL TECH CO LTD

column6: SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR PROCESSING AND MANAGING USED LITHIUM-ION BATTERIES
column7: A method for processing used lithium-ion batteries includes disassembling a used lithium-ion battery configured for a high-load application, inspecting cells of the used lithium-ion battery, charging the cells, and assessing a state-of-health of each of the cells by running a discharge test to determine an internal resistance and a capacity, comparing the internal resistance and the capacity with manufacturer specifications, and determining the state-of-health based on the comparison. The method also includes categorizing each of the cells into a reuse category, a repurpose category, or a recycle category based on the state-of-health of the cells and reusing the cells categorized in the reuse category in a battery configured for a high-load application, repurposing the cells categorized in the repurpose category into a battery configured for a low-load application, and recycling the cells categorized in the recycle category.

column6: NICKEL-RICH BATTERY RECYCLING
column7: A battery recycling process recovers lithium from nickel-rich cathode material in a recycling stream of end-of-life batteries. A dilute acid leach of a high nickel content cathode material contains a mixture of sulfuric acid based on a molar quantity of lithium in the cathode material. The highly selective leach generates a lithium rich solution with a small amount of nickel removable by nanofiltration to achieve a highly efficient recovery of the lithium contained in the recycling stream. A quantity of the leach acid based on the lithium content and a quantity of water based on a total black mass of the recycling stream results in a highly selective, near pure lithium leach when the recycling stream results from high nickel NMC batteries such as 811.

column6: Lithium battery recycling system
column7: The invention relates to the field of battery recycling, in particular to a lithium battery recycling system which comprises a conveying device, a position correcting device and a testing device. As a common rechargeable battery, the lithium battery has the advantages of high energy density, long cycle life, quick charging capability, lower self-discharge rate, environmental protection, sustainability and the like, so that the lithium battery becomes an important energy storage option in various electronic equipment and electric vehicles; however, in daily production and life, the phenomenon that the lithium batteries are not used completely often occurs, and if the lithium batteries which are not used completely can be recycled and reused in other equipment, not only can resources be saved, but also considerable economic benefits can be generated; the conveying device, the position correcting device and the testing device can be matched with one another, and batch detection of the lithium batteries is achieved under the condition that the lithium batteries are prevented from being damaged.
column8: JIANGXI ZHUOKAI NEW ENERGY TECH CO LTD

column6: Energy supply system and control strategy of lithium batteries of electric automobile
column7: The invention discloses an energy supply system and a control strategy of lithium batteries of an electric automobile, applied to energy storage and distribution of an electric automobile. Three super-capacitors are arranged between a lithium battery pack (1) and a motor and energy recycling system (2) of the automobile in parallel. With the system, the lithium batteries are less prone to large current charge-discharge impact, and thus the service life of the lithium batteries is prolonged extremely; as the life of the super-capacitors is long, the whole life of the system is prolonged, working conditions of large current discharging of the lithium batteries are reduced, and the safety of the batteries is enhanced. Current does not change greatly, so that the estimation on remaining capacity of the lithium batteries and the battery equalization are facilitated, and the algorithm complexity of a battery management system is lowered. Recycled energy is directly stored in the super-capacitors, does not need to be charged into the lithium battery pack to serve as power, and can be directly used by a motor, so that the recycled energy use ratio is enhanced, and the battery use cost is lowered.

column6: Battery packs with cell module assemblies usable in multiple applications
column7: A cell module assembly includes multiple lithium-ion battery cells connected in parallel and an electronic controller. The electronic controller is programmed to receive useful life data for a useful life indicator of the battery cells, save the life data to memory to create a life data history, determine a life measurement based on the life data history, compare the life measurement to a first end of life threshold, determine if the life measurement has met the first end of life threshold, provide a first end of life output indicating that the life measurement has met the first end of life threshold, compare the life measurement to a second end of life threshold, determine if the life measurement has met the second end of life threshold, and provide a second end of life output indicating that the life measurement has met the second end of life threshold.

column6: Lithium battery recycling equipment
column7: The invention belongs to the technical field of battery recycling, and particularly relates to lithium battery recycling equipment which comprises a recycling box body, and a feeding assembly, a cleaning assembly, a battery temporary storage assembly, an intermittent feeding assembly, a classification detection assembly, a first battery processing and collecting assembly and a second battery processing and collecting assembly are sequentially arranged in the recycling box body in the material moving direction. And the classification detection assembly is used for detecting the residual life of the lithium batteries and classifying the lithium batteries, the lithium batteries with long residual life enter the first battery processing and collecting assembly, and the lithium batteries with short residual life enter the second battery processing and collecting assembly. The lithium batteries can be classified and stored according to surplus values, resource waste is avoided, the classification process is automatic, manual inspection and sorting are not needed, manpower is saved, and meanwhile the utilization value of the lithium batteries is improved.

column6: Lithium battery echelon utilization management system
column7: The invention discloses a lithium battery echelon utilization management system which belongs to the technical field of lithium batteries and comprises a lithium battery recovery detection unit, a lithium battery evaluation unit, a new energy electric appliance investigation unit, a lithium battery installation unit and a recycling unit. According to the lithium battery echelon utilization management system provided by the invention, a lithium battery is recycled, the remaining life of the recycled lithium battery is evaluated, a lithium battery device on the market is investigated, and the secondary utilization and repeated utilization of the lithium battery are carried out according to the performance of the device and the standard of using the lithium battery, so that the function of the lithium battery is achieved to the maximum extent.

column6: METHOD FOR RECYCLING ALL TYPES OF LITHIUM BATTERIES
column7: The present disclosure discloses a method for recycling all types of lithium batteries. First, the lithium battery waste is acid-leached to obtain a solution containing most of metal ions. After filtering, the solution is separated from the remaining solids, and then the obtained solution is subjected to separate precipitation many times. After separately adjusting the pH value of the solution many times, adding precipitants with a high selectivity ratio, and matching with filtration and separation reaction, all ions in the lithium battery waste are sequentially precipitated in forms of iron phosphate (FePO4), aluminum hydroxide (Al(OH)3), manganese oxide (MnO2), dicobalt trioxide (cobalt oxide, Co2O3), nickel hydroxide (Ni(OH)2), and lithium carbonate (Li2CO3).

column6: LITHIUM BATTERY RECYCLING METHOD
column7: This application belongs to the field of battery recycling technologies, and specifically, to a lithium battery recycling method. The lithium battery recycling method includes: performing split processing on a lithium battery to obtain a metal shell and an electrode assembly respectively, where the electrode assembly includes a roll core or a lamination; and recycling the metal shell. According to the recycling method in embodiments of this application, the lithium battery is not wholly crushed, but the lithium battery is split to obtain the metal shell and the electrode assembly. In other words, the metal shell obtained through recycling by using the recycling method in this application is not crushed, thereby ensuring reusability of the metal shell.

column6: Recyclable battery for electronic cigarette
column7: The utility model discloses a recyclable battery for an electronic cigarette, and relates to the technical field of electronic cigarettes. The recyclable battery for the electronic cigarette comprises an electronic cigarette atomizer, a protective shell is attached to the surface, away from a smoke cartridge, of the electronic cigarette atomizer, and a lithium ion battery is fixedly installed in the protective shell. According to the utility model, the lithium ion battery is sleeved with the protective shell, so that the mechanical strength of the lithium ion battery can be improved, the lithium ion battery is not easy to damage in the assembly process of the electronic cigarette, the scrap of the lithium ion battery is reduced, and the lithium ion battery and the electronic cigarette are directly connected through the TYPE-C port, so that the assembly of the electronic cigarette is simple and the automation is easy to realize; the lithium ion batteries are integrally arranged to be detachable, so that the lithium ion batteries can be fully recycled, materials are fully utilized, resources are fully recycled, the lithium ion batteries in the independent batteries are easy to recycle after the service life of the lithium ion batteries expires, resource reutilization is realized, and environmental pollution is reduced.

column6: Method for preparing lithium ion battery from recyclable resources
column7: The invention provides a method for preparing a lithium ion battery from recyclable resources. By matching novel electrolyte of a lithium ion battery and recycling effective ingredients of the lithiumion battery, the resources are saved, the cost is reduced, as well as energy conservation and environmental protection area realized; and in addition, the electrolyte is stable in performance, and cyclic life is remarkably prolonged; and the method is suitable for industrial production.

column6: System and method for determining degradation of rechargeable lithium ion battery
column7: An MPU performs a degradation diagnosis based on an open circuit voltage characteristic of a rechargeable lithium ion battery indicating how the battery varies in open circuit voltage as the battery varies in capacity to obtain a capacity ratio of a positive electrode, a capacity ratio of a negative electrode, and a deviated capacity of the battery. The MPU applies the capacity ratio of the positive electrode and the capacity ratio of the negative electrode to a predetermined map for degradation attributed to wear to estimate a deviated capacity resulting from degradation attributed to wear and separates the deviated capacity into the deviated capacity resulting from degradation attributed to wear and a deviated capacity resulting from deposition of lithium. The MPU uses at least the deviated capacity resulting from deposition of lithium to determine whether a rechargeable lithium ion battery subject to determination of degradation is reusable and/or recyclable.

column6: Waste lithium battery sorting and recycling device
column7: The invention discloses a waste lithium battery sorting and recycling device, and belongs to the technical field of aluminum battery recycling, the waste lithium battery sorting and recycling device comprises a workbench, a sorting assembly and a detection assembly, the sorting assembly is arranged on the surface of the workbench, and the detection assembly is arranged above one end of the sorting assembly; and a first recycling box and a second recycling box are arranged below the detection assembly, the positive electrode orientation of the lithium battery can be kept consistent through the sorting assembly when the lithium battery is detected, the lithium battery can be conveniently detected by the detection assembly, and whether the lithium battery contains electric quantity or not is judged by detecting the lithium battery through the detection assembly. When the lithium batteries contain electricity, the detection assembly enables the lithium batteries to fall into the first recycling box, and when the lithium batteries do not contain electricity, the detection assembly enables the lithium batteries to fall into the second recycling box, and the lithium batteries containing electricity are recycled in a unified mode, so that potential safety hazards caused when some lithium batteries containing electricity are smashed and recycled are prevented.

column7: A recycled battery supply forecast unit 201 of a battery management server 20 receives, from a plurality of vehicles 40, performance information of a secondary battery 403 mounted on each of the vehicles 40 and state information of the vehicle for determining a life of the vehicle 40, makes a forecast of an end of life of the vehicle 40 by using history of the state information of that vehicle, and makes a forecast of performance of the secondary battery 403 at the end of life of that vehicle 40 by using history of the performance information of the secondary battery 403. In addition, a forecast is made of a supply quantity of recyclable secondary batteries at a certain point in time in the future based on a forecast result of the performance of the secondary battery 203 at the end of life of each vehicle 40.

column7: The utility model relates to the technical field of lithium ion batteries, and discloses a pressing device for a lithium battery module, which comprises a bottom plate, and a water circulation shell is fixedly mounted at the bottom of the bottom plate. According to the pressing device for the lithium battery module, the left side and the right side of a battery can be effectively clamped by arranging a first fixing plate, a spring and a second supporting plate, the battery of any size can be clamped through the combined action of an arranged second hydraulic rod and a third limiting block, and through an arranged multi-directional clamping mechanism, the clamping efficiency is improved. According to the lithium battery module clamping device, battery dislocation can be effectively reduced, so that time waste is reduced, a connecting pipe, a U-shaped pipe, a water tank, a condenser and a plurality of cooling fans are arranged to be used in cooperation, a large amount of heat generated in the using process of the lithium battery module clamping device is rapidly reduced in a multi-mode mode, water is recycled, and the service life of the lithium battery module clamping device is prolonged. column6: Battery replacement assisting system and server used for same
column7: A battery replacement assisting system includes a server configured to determine, from a plurality of battery packs including a recycled battery pack, a battery pack serving as a replacement candidate for a battery pack mounted on the vehicle. The server is configured to: acquire a state of deterioration of the battery pack; select a battery pack having a life longer than a period until the planned time from the plurality of battery packs by calculating a distance (life) in which the vehicle can travel until a capacity maintenance ratio of the battery pack reaches a threshold value (Qth); and determine the replacement candidate using a result of the selection and the expense information. The battery replacement assisting system further includes a display configured to notify the user of the replacement candidate determined by the server and the expense information of the replacement candidate.

column6: SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR REMOVAL AND RECYCLING OF ALUMINUM IMPURITIES FROM BATTERY WASTE
column7: Embodiments described herein relate to removal of aluminum impurities from battery waste. In some aspects, a method for removing aluminum impurities includes preprocessing a quantity of battery waste to improve removal of aluminum impurities from the quantity of battery waste. The method further includes removing at least a portion of the aluminum impurities from the quantity of battery waste, modifying the removed aluminum impurities to form a coating precursor and/or a doping precursor, and applying the coating precursor and/or the doping precursor to an electrode material. In some embodiments, the method further includes characterizing the aluminum impurities in the quantity of battery waste and regenerating the electrode material. In some embodiments, the removing can be via sieving, cyclone separation, air separation, elutriation, and/or dissolution. In some embodiments, the doping precursor can include aluminum hydroxide (Al(OH)3). In some embodiments, the regenerating includes applying a heat treatment to the electrode material.

column6: Echelon utilization lithium battery performance evaluation method
column7: The invention discloses an echelon utilization lithium battery performance evaluation method which can be applied to echelon recycling of lithium batteries. The echelon utilization lithium battery performance evaluation method comprises the steps of designing performance test condition of a lithium battery, that is, designing the test time, determining the initial SOC state and designing the discharge intermittent time; extracting health feature data of the lithium battery according to the designed test conditions; then performing SOH estimation based on a health state decision-making method of a multi-health life model data fusion technology; and finally dividing an echelon utilization range of the lithium battery. The echelon utilization lithium battery performance evaluation method can be applied to echelon recycling of the lithium battery, not only can enable the performance of the lithium battery to be given into full play so as to be conducive to energy conservation and emission reduction, but also can relieve pressure brought for the recycling work by a circumstance that a great number of lithium batteries get into a recycling stage.

column6: Method for manufacturing lithium-containing composite oxide
column7: To simply manufacture a lithium-containing oxide at lower manufacturing cost. A method for manufacturing a lithium-containing composite oxide expressed by a general formula LiMPO 4 (M is one or more of Fe (II), Mn (II), Co (II), and Ni (II)). A solution containing Li and P is formed and then is dripped in a solution containing M (M is one or more of Fe (II), Mn (II), Co (II), and Ni (II)) to form a mixed solution. By a hydrothermal method using the mixed solution, a single crystal particle of a lithium-containing composite oxide expressed by the general formula LiMPO 4 (M is one or more of Fe (II), Mn (II), Co (II), and Ni (II)) is manufactured.

column6: Battery Recycling Method
column7: Methods are proposed for extracting transition metal oxides from scrap batteries by dissolving the metal oxides in a glass-forming oxide melt, followed by electrolytic reduction of the transition metal onto the cathode of an electrolytic cell. Suitable glass-forming oxide melts include borate and pyrophosphate melts with added Na2O or NaF. The method is particularly suited to the recovery of cobalt, nickel, and manganese from scrap battery and electronic materials. column6: METHOD AND ELECTROCHEMICAL SYSTEM FOR RECYCLING SPENT LITHIUM-ION BATTERY
column7: The present invention discloses a method for recycling a spent lithium-ion battery, including the following steps: sandwiching a cathode of the spent lithium-ion battery with a conductive acid-resistant material as a cathode of a primary battery system; sandwiching an anode of the spent lithium-ion battery with a conductive acid-resistant material as an anode of the primary battery system; injecting an acid solution into a chamber of the primary battery system; and carrying out, after an electrochemical reaction is completed, solid-liquid separation on a mixed liquor in the chamber. The present invention further discloses an electrochemical system for recycling a spent lithium-ion battery. The method for recycling a spent lithium-ion battery in the present invention requires only dismantlement of cathode and anode materials, without a series of complex pretreatment operations on the cathode materials of the spent lithium-ion battery. In addition, by the method, the cathodes and anodes of the spent lithium-ion battery can be recycled at the same time, and valuable elements can be separated, which is greatly improved compared with the electrolytic leaching method. Moreover, there is no need to add an external power supply, which saves energy and can also output electricity.

column6: METHOD FOR RECYCLING LITHIUM-ION SECONDARY BATTERY
column7: Provided is a method for recycling a lithium-ion secondary battery, which includes: providing a used lithium-ion secondary battery that includes: (i) a battery element including a positive electrode, a separator, and a negative electrode, wherein at least one of the positive electrode and the negative electrode is a ceramic electrode; (ii) an electrolytic solution; and (iii) a battery container accommodating the battery element and the electrolytic solution; taking out the ceramic electrode from the lithium-ion secondary battery so that the positive electrode and the negative electrode are separated from each other, provided that the separator may be bonded to the ceramic electrode taken out; subjecting the ceramic electrode taken out to an electrode restoration treatment including cleaning and/or heat treatment; and putting the ceramic electrode subjected to the electrode restoration treatment back into the battery container to assemble a lithium-ion secondary battery.

column6: Recycling method of decommissioned lithium nickel cobalt manganese oxide battery cathode graphite
column7: The invention relates to the field of recycling of lithium ion battery resources, in particular to a recycling method of decommissioned lithium nickel cobalt manganese oxide battery negative electrode graphite. The method comprises the steps of ultrasonic powder removal, heat treatment, acid leaching reaction, washing, coating modification of modified hard carbon resin and the like on a graphite negative plate of the decommissioned nickel cobalt lithium manganate battery, so that impurities in graphite powder can be effectively removed, and surface defects of the graphite powder can be repaired; the discharge capacity and the first efficiency are improved, and the efficient recycling of the nickel cobalt lithium manganate battery negative electrode graphite is realized. The method provided by the invention not only can effectively realize the recycling of the decommissioned lithium nickel cobalt manganese oxide battery cathode graphite, but also can improve the purity and performance of the lithium ion battery cathode graphite powder, increase the cycle life and energy density of the battery, and has good economic and environmental benefits.

column6: COMPREHENSIVE RECYCLING METHOD FOR WASTE LITHIUM IRON PHOSPHATE BATTERIES
column7: A comprehensive recycling method for a waste lithium iron phosphate battery relates to a waste lithium ion battery recycling technology, and particularly comprises: first selectively extracting lithium, and then using a lithium extraction residue to prepare iron phosphate, the using the lithium extraction residue to prepare the iron phosphate comprising: adding the lithium extraction residue to water to form a slurry, adding hydrochloric acid and stirring to react, so that iron is completely dissolved, performing solid-liquid separation, on the basis of iron and phosphorus contents of the obtained liquid, adding trisodium phosphate or ferric chloride, and then adding a sodium hydroxide solution to precipitate crude iron phosphate; and then performing reverse three-stage washing to remove impurities to obtain a battery iron phosphate product. The method has a simple process flow and low material consumption, a recycling rate of the iron phosphate of more than 93% and an amount of waste water reduced by more than 75%, solves the problem of environmental protection and recycles all of the valuable elements at the same time, greatly reduces a relative cost by about 25%, and is applied to industrial applications.

column6: Cascade lithium ion battery recycling method and system
column7: The invention relates to an echelon lithium ion battery recycling method and system, and belongs to the technical field of lithium ion battery recycling. In the charging and discharging process of the recycled echelon lithium ion battery, the charging cut-off voltage is lower than the standard charging cut-off voltage and/or the set discharging cut-off voltage is higher than the standard discharging cut-off voltage by limiting the charging and discharging voltage of the echelon battery. By limiting the charging and discharging voltage of the echelon battery, the volume expansion and shrinkage of the positive and negative electrode materials in the lithium embedding/removing process can be inhibited to a certain extent, and the generated stress can be relieved, so that the electrode materials are not easy to crack or strip from a current collector; and the cycle life of the echelon lithium ion battery can be prolonged.

column6: METHOD FOR RECYCLING ELECTRODE MATERIALS OF LITHIUM ION BATTERIES
column7: A method for recycling electrode materials of lithium ion batteries, including the following steps: (1) disassembling the waste lithium ion battery to get positive electrode and negative electrode, immersing the positive electrode and/or the negative electrode into ammonia, then washing by deionized water and drying the positive electrode and/or the negative electrode; (2) sintering the dried positive electrode and/or the negative electrode, and using mechanical method to separate electrode powder material from current collector to get positive electrode powder material and/or negative electrode powder material; (3) supplementing lithium to the positive electrode powder material, then processing the positive electrode powder material by milling, spray drying and sintering to obtain regenerated positive electrode material; or processing the negative electrode powder material by milling, spray drying and sintering to obtain regenerated negative electrode material. column6: Waste lithium ion battery recycling method and device
column7: The invention relates to the field of lithium ion batteries, in particular to a waste lithium ion battery recycling method and device, electronic equipment and a computer readable storage medium. The waste lithium ion battery recycling method comprises the steps that waste lithium ion batteries are pretreated to obtain first battery broken materials, and the first battery broken materials comprise electrolytes of the waste lithium ion batteries; the first battery crushed object is subjected to first cracking, a second battery crushed object and first tail gas are obtained, the second battery crushed object comprises lithium fluoride, and the first tail gas comprises phosphorus pentafluoride; introducing the first tail gas into absorption liquid to obtain hexafluorophosphate; and carrying out post-treatment on the second crushed battery material to obtain a first recycled material. Therefore, the problem that the electrolyte of the waste lithium ion battery cannot be completely recycled is solved, the recycling benefit is improved, and overhigh tail gas treatment cost and environmental pollution are avoided. The invention further provides a waste lithium ion battery recycling device, electronic equipment and a computer readable storage medium.

column6: Method for prolonging residual recycling service life of in-parallel lithium ion battery cupboard
column7: The invention discloses a method for prolonging residual recycling service life of an in-parallel lithium ion battery cupboard, and aims to prolong the residual recycling service life of a battery cupboard on the premise that the loading power demand is met. According to the technical scheme, an in-parallel lithium ion battery cupboard discharge current optimal control system consisting of N+1 current measuring meters, N direct current-direct current converters, a computer and an internal resistance measuring meter is firstly established. After a certain period, the in-parallel lithium ion battery cupboard discharge current optimal control system detects the load current request Itotal, estimates the size of an SOH value of each module, and distributes the discharge current of each module according to the principle that a module with good property discharges large current and a module with poor property discharges small current on premise that a formula as described in the description is guaranteed, so as to slow down the SOH attenuation tendency of the module with poor property. Due to the adoption of the method, all modules can simultaneously reach the expected ending point of the recycling service life, the property of each module can be taken into play well, and the residual recycling service life of the lithium ion battery cupboard can be maximized.

column6: Optimizing battery use for known future load
column7: Methods for optimizing battery use for a known future load are described. In an embodiment, one or more battery cells are selected from a set of battery cells to provide power to a computing-based device. The battery cells are selected based on discharge profile data for each battery cell and both the current power requirement and a known future power requirement of the computing-based device. The known future power requirement is calculated based on information available to the operating system running on the computing-based device. In some examples, one or more battery cells may also be selected for charging when a power source is available and these cells may be selected based on charge profile data and the known future power requirement. The selection of the battery cells may also be made in order to satisfy a battery optimization goal, which may be defined by a user.

column6: Methods for online estimation of battery capacity and state of health
column7: In some variations, a method of real-time monitoring of battery capacity comprises correlating electrode open-circuit voltage with electrode state of charge for a selected electrode; compiling a look-up table to correlate the electrode open-circuit voltage with the electrode capacity at different values of the active-material capacity; during real-time operation, identifying first and second times at which battery terminal voltages are approximated as battery open-circuit voltages; and calculating battery capacity based on the difference in battery open-circuit voltages at the first and second times, current integration, and the look-up table. No reference electrode is needed, and a complete battery charge/discharge is not necessary to determine the capacity. This technique can therefore be implemented on-board and in real time to provide reliable capacity estimation even as the battery ages. The methods are applicable to various metal-ion secondary battery systems, including lithium-ion batteries, with different material chemistries.

column6: Early-Life Diagnostics For Fast Battery Formation Protocols And Their Impacts To Long-Term Aging
column7: The present disclosure relates to a method for optimizing the formation protocol of a battery. The method can include the steps of: (a) providing a battery cell structure comprising an anode, an electrolyte, and a cathode including cations that move from the cathode to the anode during charging; (b) performing a first charge of the battery cell structure using a predetermined formation protocol to create a formed battery cell; and (c) determining a cell internal resistance of the formed battery cell. Therefore, one can compare the cell internal resistances of two battery cells formed by using identical battery cell structures and different formation protocols, and select a formation protocol if the first cell internal resistance of a first formed battery is greater than or less than the second cell internal resistance of a second formed battery.

column6: Aerogel-based electrodes
column7: Electrodes, production methods and mono-cell batteries are provided, which comprise active material particles embedded in electrically conductive metallic porous structure, dry-etched anode structures and battery structures with thick anodes and cathodes that have spatially uniform resistance. The metallic porous structure provides electric conductivity, a large volume that supports good ionic conductivity, that in turn reduces directional elongation of the particles during operation, and may enable reduction or removal of binders, conductive additives and/or current collectors to yield electrodes with higher structural stability, lower resistance, possibly higher energy density and longer cycling lifetime. Dry etching treatments may be used to reduce oxidized surfaces of the active material particles, thereby simplifying production methods and enhancing porosity and ionic conductivity of the electrodes. Electrodes may be made thick and used to form mono-cell batteries which are simple to produce and yield high performance.

Both the FC and the SCeFC have anodes based on the same anode active material, and the SCeFC is configured to operate at high rates within a limited operation range of state of charge (SoC), maintained by the control unit, which is further configured to manage the FC and the SCeFC with respect to power delivery to and from the EV, respectively, and manage power delivery from the SCeFC to the FC according to specified criteria that minimize a depth of discharge and/or a number of cycles of the FC.

column7: The present invention provides a fast charging method for battery, including the steps as follows: (1) the battery is charged with a constant current I1 and the charging time is t1; (2) the battery is charged with a constant current I2 and the charging time is t2; (3) the battery is discharged with the constant current I2 and the discharging time is t3, recycling until the voltage reaches a pre-charging voltage of battery; (4) the battery is standed after the voltage reaching the pre-charging voltage of battery, and the rest time is t4, and then the battery is charged with a constant current I3 until the voltage reaching a cut-off voltage of battery, and then the battery is charged with a constant voltage until the current reaching a cut-off current I4; wherein, I2<I1, t2<t1, t3<t1, I2<I3≰I1, I4≰I2. The fast charging method provided in present invention can decrease the polarization in pulse charging phase, increase the time of whole pulse charging phase, improve the charging speed in pulse charging phase, and thus shorten the full-charging time.

column6: Modulated, temperature-based multi-CC-CV charging technique for Li-ion/Li-polymer batteries
column7: Some embodiments of the present invention provide a system that charges a battery. During operation, the system obtains a set of charging currents {I1, . . . , In} and a set of charging voltages {V1, . . . , Vn}. Next, the system repeats constant-current and constant-voltage charging operations, starting with i=1 and incrementing i with every repetition, until a termination condition is reached. These constant-current and constant-voltage charging operations involve charging the battery using a constant current Ii until a cell voltage of the battery reaches Vi, and then charging the battery using a constant voltage Vi until a charging current is less than or equal to Ii+1.

column6: Supercapacitor-emulating fast-charging batteries and devices
column7: Methods and supercapacitor-emulating fast-charging batteries are provided. Devices are provided, which comprise control circuitry and a modified fast-charging lithium ion battery having Si, Ge and/or Sn-based anode active material and designed to operate at 5 C at least and within a range of 5% at most around a working point of between 60-80% lithiation of the Si, Ge and/or Sn-based anode active material, wherein the control circuitry is configured to maintain a state of charge (SOC) of the battery within the operation range around the working point.

column6: A Lithium ion battery charging method and a cycle performance testing method thereof

The invention also provides a testing method for the cycling performance of the lithium ion battery. The testing method is adapted to the charging method and adopts the static state- Discharge- Static state-charging cycle performance of lithium batteries is tested. The charging current is set on the basis of the previous discharge capacity of lithium batteries. As that char current in the invention is based on the available capacity of the lithium battery, At present, that problem that the char rate increases due to the char parameter determined by the rated capacity is avoided, At that same time, the char method ofthe invention is also applicable to the field of fast charging, and the char method of the invention is introduced under the fast charging step, so that the fast char cycle performance of the lithiumbattery can be improved.

column6: LOW TEMPERATURE FAST CHARGE
column7: An automated charge preparation method periodically determines critical parameters for the set of relevant operating conditions, determines whether fast charging is possible, applies fast charging when possible, otherwise applies a dynamically scaled charging rate that is optimized based upon current critical parameters (while optionally heating the individual battery cells as long as fast charging is not available) to reduce/eliminate a risk of lithium-plating.

column6: Lithium ion battery lossless fast charging method and system based on multi-frequency sine nonlinear characterization
column7: The invention provides a lithium ion battery lossless fast charging method and system based on multi-frequency sine nonlinear characterization, and relates to the technical field of battery charging optimization. According to the lossless fast charging method, non-linear characterization of the lithium ion battery is carried out based on a dynamic in-situ multi-frequency sine impedance spectrum; the frequency domain voltage nonlinear response of the lithium ion battery is decomposed into even order and odd order responses to obtain a lithium precipitation boundary when the battery is charged; and designing a lithium ion battery lossless fast charging strategy based on the lithium precipitation boundary: dividing lithium ion battery charging into a pre-charging stage, a fast charging stage and a floating charging stage, and executing a preset charging strategy in each stage. The lithium precipitation boundary is determined based on the dynamic in-situ multi-frequency sine impedance spectroscopy, so that lithium precipitation is avoided while the battery is rapidly charged, the charging time is shortened, the safety is improved, and the battery is effectively protected.

column6: AEROGEL-BASED ELECTRODES
column7: Electrodes, production methods and mono-cell batteries are provided, which comprise active material particles embedded in electrically conductive metallic porous structure, dry-etched anode structures and battery structures with thick anodes and cathodes that have spatially uniform resistance. The metallic porous structure provides electric conductivity, a large volume that supports good ionic conductivity, that in turn reduces directional elongation of the particles during operation, and may enable reduction or removal of binders, conductive additives and/or current collectors to yield electrodes with higher structural stability, lower resistance, possibly higher energy density and longer cycling lifetime. Dry etching treatments may be used to reduce oxidized surfaces of the active material particles, thereby simplifying production methods and enhancing porosity and ionic conductivity of the electrodes. Electrodes may be made thick and used to form mono-cell batteries which are simple to produce and yield high performance.

column6: Fast-charging lithium battery with real-time voltage detection function
column7: The utility model discloses a fast-charging lithium battery with a real-time voltage detection function, and relates to the technical field of fast-charging lithium batteries, the fast-charging lithium battery comprises a battery block, the periphery of the battery block is sleeved with a shell, and the upper end of the shell is provided with a detection assembly. The problems that the voltage change in the battery cannot be clearly known due to the fact that the capacitance in the battery is not known, the function of the fast-charging lithium battery is insufficient or the endurance time is short when the charging time is too long, the battery is collided when the charging time is too long, and even the battery is exploded when the battery is seriously collided are solved. According to the utility model, through the arrangement of the battery block, the first conductive foil, the second conductive foil, the detection assembly, the chip, the LED lamp and the voltage stabilizing diode, real-time voltage detection of the fast-charging lithium battery is realized.

column6: Magnetic field-assisted fast charging of lithium-ion batteries
column7: Fast charging of a lithium-ion battery at 4C rate or 5C rate or more is improved by applying an external magnetic field relative to the battery to establish magnetic field lines that extend in a direction of primary movement of lithium ions toward the graphite anode during fast charging. Deleterious degradation of the graphite anode from repeated fast charging can be reduced or eliminated by practice of the invention.

column6: METHODS FOR FAST-CHARGING AND DETECTING LITHIUM PLATING IN LITHIUM ION BATTERIES
column7: Methods for fast-charging batteries while minimizing lithium plating (LP) comprise charging the battery in a first phase at a near-maximum charging current, subsequently charging the battery in a second phase by decreasing the charging current while charging in order to maintain the anode potential equal to or above an anode potential threshold, and subsequently charging the battery in a third phase at constant cell potential such that the cathode potential remains below a cathode potential threshold. LP can be detected by determining the derivative of the charging current and examining the derivative for smooth curves or local discontinuities, wherein a smooth curve indicates the absence of LP and a curve with a local discontinuity indicates the presence of LP. A fast-charging profile can be defined by plotting the cell potential vs. the charging current from the first phase, the second phase, and the third phase to define a fast-charging profile.

column6: BATTERY CHARGING METHOD AND APPARATUS
column7: In a method of charging a battery, a charging control device obtains a battery parameter that includes an electrode parameter of the battery and one or more of a structure parameter, a manufacturing process parameter, an electrical parameter, an electrolyte parameter, a diaphragm parameter, and a thermophysical parameter of the battery. The charging control device inputs the battery parameter input into a battery model represented by an ordinary differential equation to obtain a safe charging boundary value of the battery in n cycles, where n is greater than or equal to 2 and less than or equal to N, N is a cycle life of the battery, and the n cycles refer to n cycles selected from 0 to N cycles. The safe charging boundary value is a maximum charging current in which no lithium plating occurs on the battery in different states of charge SOCs and at different temperatures.

column6: High-voltage electrolyte suitable for fast-charging lithium battery and lithium battery

The fluoronitrile compound is used as the solvent of the lithium battery electrolyte, so that the electrolyte not only ensures the wide electrochemical window and ultrahigh ionic conductivity of the nitrile electrolyte, but also ensures the excellent film-forming property and low interface impedance of the fluoride electrolyte, and meanwhile, the huge progress of the lithium battery in the fields of high voltage and fast charge is realized. The lithium salt, the fluoronitrile compound and the additive in the high-voltage fast-charging electrolyte for the lithium battery are specifically combined, and the concentration and the ratio are further optimized, so that the high-voltage fast-charging electrolyte added with the high-voltage fast-charging electrolyte has excellent compatibility with a positive electrode and a negative electrode, and the long cycle life and the high coulombic efficiency of the lithium battery are further realized.

column6: PREPARATION METHOD OF SOFT CARBON AND LITHIUM-ION SECONDARY BATTERY
column7: A preparation method of soft carbon comprises heating heavy oil to raw coke under a condition of 480° C.-550° C., heating the raw coke to 850° C.-900° C. at a first heating rate of 3° C./min to 5° C./min and holding the temperature for 4 hours or more to obtain a carbon-containing material, grinding and grading the carbon-containing material to obtain a carbon-containing powder, heating the carbon-containing powder to 1030° C.-1220° C. at a second heating rate of 3° C./min to 10° C./min and holding the temperature for 4 hours or more to obtain a carbon material powder, and adding pitch to the carbon material powder, and then heating it to 1030° C.-1220° C. at a third heating rate of 0.90° C./min to 1.25° C./min and holding the temperature for 5 hours or more to obtain soft carbon.

column6: Method and device for fast-charging of rechargeable batteries
column7: A system and method for fast charging of a lithium-ion battery, including: continuously monitoring a state of charge (SOC) of the lithium-ion battery; during a normal mode of operation and upon detecting that the battery is at the predetermined low charge level, discontinuing the discharge; upon detecting that the battery is connected to a charger, providing charging rate of at least 4 C for at least part of charging; and upon detecting that the battery, while connected to the charger is at the predetermined high charge level, discontinue the charging, wherein the predetermined low charge level and the predetermined high charge level define a consumable capacity of the battery, wherein the consumable capacity is below 50% of the full capacity of the battery.

column6: Battery lithium precipitation protection method and device, electronic equipment and storage medium
column7: The invention discloses a battery lithium precipitation protection method and device, electronic equipment and a storage medium. The battery lithium precipitation protection method comprises the steps that the charging and discharging cycle life and the quick charging time of a battery in a preset quick charging mode are acquired; according to the cycle life and the fast charging time, determining the number of cycles of charging and discharging in a preset fast charging mode; determining a current rate in a trimming mode according to the fast charging time, wherein the current rate in the trimming mode is smaller than a current rate in a preset fast charging mode; and converting the charging mode of the battery from a preset fast charging mode to a finishing mode according to the cycle index. By adding the trimming mode after the multi-time fast charging mode, lithium precipitation of the battery can be prevented or slowed down. Moreover, the cycle life represents the fast charge cycle performance of the battery, the fast charge time of the preset fast charge mode represents the severity of lithium precipitation of the battery caused by the preset fast charge mode, and the cycle times of charging and discharging in the preset fast charge mode are determined by combining the cycle life and the fast charge time, so that the setting of the cycle times is more reasonable; and the influence on user experience is avoided.

column6: PREPARATION METHOD OF SOFT CARBON AND LITHIUM-ION SECONDARY BATTERY
column7: A preparation method of soft carbon comprises heating heavy oil to raw coke under a condition of 480°C-550°C, heating the raw coke to 850°C-900°C at a first heating rate of 3°C/min to 5°C/min and holding the temperature for 4 hours or more to obtain a carbon-containing material, grinding and grading the carbon-containing material to obtain a carbon-containing powder, heating the carbon-containing powder to 1030°C-1220°C at a second heating rate of 3°C/min to 10°C/min and holding the temperature for 4 hours or more to obtain a carbon material powder,; and adding pitch to the carbon material powder, and then heating it to 1030°C-1220°C at a third heating rate of 0.90°C/min to 1.25°C/min and holding the temperature for 5 hours or more to obtain soft carbon.

column6: Formulating method and application of battery lithium precipitation-free fast charging system
column7: The invention provides a formulating method and application of a battery lithium precipitation-free fast charging system, and relates to the technical field of lithium ion batteries. The method for formulating the lithium precipitation-free fast charging system of the battery comprises the following steps: firstly, detecting the cathode potential of the battery in a stepped charging process through three electrodes, and judging whether the cathode potential lt exists or not; if the negative electrode potential is 0 V, the negative electrode potential is 1t; if the lithium precipitation risk exists under the condition of 0 V, the rapid charging process step is corrected through the depolarization treatment step, it is ensured that the lithium precipitation risk does not exist in the rapid charging process of the battery, then capacity diving caused by lithium precipitation does not occur in the battery circulation process, meanwhile, the total duration needed by rapid charging is not obviously increased through the rapid charging system, and the rapid charging efficiency is improved. And the requirements on performance and fast charging time can be met. The invention further provides application of the formulating method of the battery lithium precipitation-free fast charging system in the field of lithium ion batteries.

Fast-charging lithium battery with heat dissipation and explosion-proof functions
column7: The utility model discloses a quick-charge lithium battery with heat dissipation and explosion-proof functions, which relates to the technical field of quick-charge lithium batteries and comprises a quick-charge lithium battery main body and a shell, the quick-charge lithium battery main body is arranged in the shell, a heat dissipation component is arranged in the shell, and an anti-seismic component is arranged in the shell. The utility model solves the problems that the heat dissipation effect of the fast-charging lithium battery is poor, the heat generated when the lithium battery is used cannot be quickly dissipated, and the fast-charging lithium battery is easy to swell and explode due to high temperature and overheating because the shell of the existing fast-charging lithium battery is tightly attached to the lithium battery main body. column6: Stepped fast charging method and system for effectively improving cycle life of lithium ion battery
column7: The invention belongs to the technical field of lithium battery testing, and particularly relates to a stepped fast charging method and system for effectively improving the cycle life of a lithium ion battery. The method comprises the following steps: setting a plurality of charging currents and a plurality of charging voltages; performing iterative alternating constant-current and constant-voltage charging on the lithium-ion battery according to the charging current and the charging voltage until the current of the lithium-ion battery is reduced to a target current; wherein the execution sequence of the charging current is a descending sequence. According to the invention, the whole fast charging process is divided into three sections of constant-current and constant-voltage charging, the characteristic current is identified at the high SOC, the charging current of the battery to the full-charge state after the fast charging is completed is relieved, the requirement of 0-80% SOC fast charging is met, the high-rate charging of the high SOC is reduced, and the fast charging service life of the lithium ion battery is prolonged. In addition, a BMS system which does not support lithium ion battery SOC identification can be satisfied, the fast charging SOC interval can be simply and conveniently controlled through voltage control, and complex SOC identification is not needed.

The preparation method comprises the following steps: step 1, preparing artificial graphite; step 2, preparing graphene modified asphalt powder E; and step 3, coating artificial graphite with the graphene modified asphalt to prepare the graphite negative electrode material for rapid charging of the lithium battery. The invention also provides the lithium battery fast charging graphite negative electrode material prepared by the method. The lithium battery fast charging graphite negative electrode material prepared by the method has relatively high discharge capacity and first efficiency, and meanwhile, the fast charging performance of the lithium battery fast charging graphite negative electrode material is excellent.

column6: Two-phase immersed battery liquid cooling device for super fast charging of lithium battery and cooling system of two-phase immersed battery liquid cooling device
column7: The invention belongs to the technical field of power batteries, and provides a two-phase immersed battery liquid cooling device for super fast charging of a lithium battery and a cooling system of the two-phase immersed battery liquid cooling device. According to the device, the electric heating film is adopted to preheat the lithium battery before fast charging, and then the phase change latent heat of the fluorinated liquid is utilized to take away heat generated in the fast charging process of the battery, so that the temperature rise of the battery in the fast charging process can be effectively limited at a relatively high initial temperature; and the temperature of the battery can be accurately controlled to be close to the boiling point of the fluorinated liquid, so that the thermal safety of the lithium battery in the fast charging process is greatly improved. And after the fast charging is finished, the high-temperature fluorination liquid in the battery box is pumped into the liquid storage tank by using the electromagnetic pump, and normal-temperature liquid is injected into the battery box, so that the temperature of the lithium battery is quickly reduced to the room temperature level after the fast charging is finished, the growth of SEI in the battery during the subsequent discharging or storage period is inhibited, and the cycle life of the lithium ion battery is greatly prolonged.

column6: Lithium manganate battery mobile power supply
column7: The utility model discloses a lithium manganate battery mobile power supply, comprising a charging device for realizing fast charging based on a lithium manganate cell, the charging device comprises amain control chip, a lithium manganate cell and an interface circuit, the interface circuit comprises a 3C fast charger, the 3C fast charger is connected with the main control chip for fast chargingthe lithium manganate cell, and the interface circuit is connected with the main control chip for fast charging the lithium manganate cell. And the main control chip is connected with the lithium manganate cell. According to the utility model, the lithium manganate battery is used as a power supply core of the fast-charging mobile power supply, and the charging time can be compressed to 20 minutesin cooperation with the fast charger.

column6: Intelligent charging method for lithium ion power battery
column7: The invention discloses an intelligent charging method for a lithium ion power battery. The intelligent charging method comprises the following steps: constructing a battery fast charging simulation model and acquiring parameters; acquiring an initial charging safety boundary of the battery; verifying the initial charging safety boundary through a lithium ion power battery full life cycle data test platform to obtain a target charging safety boundary; according to the target charging safety boundary, calculating a final optimal solution of multiple targets of battery fast charging through an improved genetic algorithm to obtain a first fast charging strategy; the first fast charging strategy is optimized according to the pulse width, a final target fast charging strategy is obtained, and the final target fast charging strategy obtained through the overall steps can shorten the charging time of the lithium ion power battery, reduce temperature rise, delay the service life of the battery and reduce energy loss during charging. The method can be widely applied to the technical field of rapid battery charging optimization.

column6: Battery charging and power utilization equipment
column7: The embodiment of the invention provides a charging method of a battery and electric equipment, and the charging method provided by the embodiment of the invention can eliminate polarization caused by large-current fast charging and improve interface lithium precipitation under the condition of slightly increasing the charging time by carrying out large-current and small-current alternate charging and discharging circulation in a fast charging circulation process, so that the charging efficiency of the battery is improved, and the service life of the battery is prolonged. The abnormal diving phenomenon of the battery cell is prevented, and the interface SOC regional difference caused by fast charging is balanced; in addition, low-current charging can reduce the temperature rise difference of the battery core, reduce the regional over-charge and over-discharge phenomena, improve the lithium precipitation and prolong the available life of the battery, so that the problem that the lithium precipitation of the negative electrode of the lithium battery cannot be improved on the premise of ensuring the fast charging performance in the existing charging mode is solved.

column6: Fast-charging lithium battery negative electrode material and preparation method thereof
column7: The invention discloses a fast-charging lithium battery negative electrode material and a preparation method thereof. The preparation method of the fast-charging lithium battery negative electrode material comprises the following steps: (1) heating and melting asphalt and resin, homogenizing, and cooling to obtain a precursor A; (2) pre-carbonizing the precursor A under the protection of N2; (3) crushing and grading; and (4) graphitizing to obtain the required fast-charging lithium battery negative electrode material. The specific surface area of the fast-charging lithium battery negative electrode material is 1-3m < 2 >/g, and the tap density is greater than or equal to 1.0 g/cc; the first reversible capacity of a 0.1 C half battery can reach 320mAh/g, and the first coulombic efficiency is greater than or equal to 91%; the fast-charging lithium battery negative electrode material integrates the characteristics of high capacity and high initial efficiency of graphite and fast charging capability of hard carbon, and meets the use requirements of the fast-charging lithium battery negative electrode material; raw materials are wide in source, the process is simple, and industrial production is met.
column8: JIANGXI ZHENGTUO NEW ENERGY TECH CO LTD

column6: Rapid evaluation method for cycle performance of rapidly-charged graphite of lithium ion battery
column7: The invention relates to a rapid evaluation method for cycle performance of fast-charge graphite of a lithium ion battery, and the method specifically comprises the following steps: (1) at normal temperature, firstly discharging electricity for a standard fast-charge battery and a fast-charge battery to be tested, then fully charging with large current, after fully charging, fully standing until the voltage is unchanged, and respectively recording the voltage drop of the two batteries; (2) performing high-temperature cycle test on the standard fast-charging battery and the fast-charging battery to be tested; and (3) by comparing the voltage drop and high-temperature cycle performance of the standard fast charging battery and the fast charging battery to be tested, evaluating and judging the cycle performance of the two types of fast charging graphite. According to the rapid evaluation method for the cycle performance of the fast-charging graphite of the lithium ion battery, the fast-charging graphite can be rapidly screened and evaluated, the evaluation period of the fast-charging graphite is shortened, and the method is simple and easy to operate and implement.

column6: Internally adjustable modular single battery systems for power systems
column7: Single, internally adjustable modular battery systems are provided, for handling power delivery from and to various power systems such as electric vehicles, photovoltaic systems, solar systems, grid-scale battery energy storage systems, home energy storage systems and power walls. Batteries comprise a main fast-charging lithium ion battery (FC), configured to deliver power to the electric vehicle, a supercapacitor-emulating fast-charging lithium ion battery (SCeFC), configured to receive power and deliver power to the FC and/or to the EV and to operate at high rates within a limited operation range of state of charge (SoC), respective module management systems, and a control unit. Both the FC and the SCeFC have anodes based on the same anode active material and the control unit is configured to manage the FC and the SCeFC and manage power delivery to and from the power system(s), to optimize the operation of the FC.

column6: Controller for lithium ion secondary battery that sets and modifies a lower limit state of charge, and vehicle

column6: METHOD FOR ACTIVATION OF LITHIUM SECONDARY BATTERY

column6: BATTERY CHARGING METHOD AND DEVICE
column7: A battery charging method is provided. In the method, a battery parameter is first obtained, where the battery parameter includes an electrode parameter of a battery. Then, the battery parameter is input into a battery model represented by an ordinary differential equation, to obtain a safe charging boundary value of the battery in n cycles, where the parameter further includes one or more of a structure parameter, a manufacturing process parameter, an electrical parameter, an electrolyte parameter, a diaphragm parameter, and a thermophysical parameter of the battery. The safe charging boundary value is a maximum charging current in which no lithium plating occurs on the battery in different states of charge SOCs and at different temperatures, where n is greater than or equal to 2 and less than or equal to N, N is a cycle life of the battery, and the n cycles refer to n cycles selected from 0 to N cycles. Finally, a maximum safe charging current value in a current quantity of cycles and in a current SOC of the battery is obtained based on the current quantity of cycles, a current temperature, the current SOC, and the safe charging boundary value of the battery.

column6: Preparation method of negative electrode powder of fast-charging type lithium ion battery and application of negative electrode powder in lithium ion battery
column7: The invention discloses a preparation method of negative electrode powder of a fast-charging type lithium ion battery and application of the negative electrode powder in a lithium ion battery. The method comprises the following steps: step 1, preparing amorphous carbon particles; step 2, preparing graphite particles; step 3, preparing carbon particles with a porous double-layer structure; and step 4, preparing the negative electrode powder of the fast-charging type lithium ion battery. The negative electrode powder of the fast-charging type lithium ion battery prepared by the method has a multi-layer nested structure in the form of soft carbon-graphite-hard carbon or hard carbon-graphite-soft carbon and the like, has good compatibility with an electrolyte, multiple lithium intercalation channels and a high lithium ion intercalation speed; and the fast-charging type lithium ion battery prepared from the negative electrode powder can realize 3-8 C fast charging capacity, has constant-current charging capacity of greater than or equal to 70%, 3C charge-discharge cycle life of greater than or equal to 1500 times and 8C charge/1C discharge cycle life of greater than or equal to 500 times, and the can meet the quick charging requirement of a digital lithium ion battery and the requirement of a new energy vehicle on the quick charging capacity of the lithium ion battery.

column6: ELECTRIC VEHICLES WITH ADAPTIVE FAST-CHARGING, UTILIZING SUPERCAPACITOR-EMULATING BATTERIES
column7: Electric vehicles (EVs), power trains and control units and methods are provided. Power trains comprise a main fast-charging lithium ion battery (FC), configured to deliver power to the electric vehicle, a supercapacitor-emulating fast-charging lithium ion battery (SCeFC), configured to receive power and deliver power to the FC and/or to the EV, and a control unit. Both the FC and the SCeFC have anodes based on the same anode active material, and the SCeFC is configured to operate at high rates within a limited operation range of state of charge (SoC), maintained by the control unit, which is further configured to manage the FC and the SCeFC with respect to power delivery to and from the EV, respectively, and manage power delivery from the SCeFC to the FC according to specified criteria that minimize a depth of discharge and/or a number of cycles of the FC.

column6: Sectional charging method of lithium ion battery
column7: The invention discloses a sectional charging method of a lithium ion battery. The method applied to the lithium ion battery with a lithium ion battery protector or a lithium ion battery pack with the lithium ion battery protector includes: after charging is started, the lithium ion battery is constantly charged by using a first current set value; when the voltage value in the lithium ion battery reaches a first voltage set value, a charger weakens the charging currents to a second current set value to averagely charge the lithium ion battery or the lithium ion battery pack; when the voltage value in the lithium ion battery reaches a second voltage set value, the charger further weakens the charging currents to a third current set value to floatingly charge the lithium ion battery or the lithium ion battery pack until the voltage value in the lithium ion battery reaches the highest voltage value. The method has the advantages that the physical protecting switch and current balancing ability of the lithium ion battery protector and sectional charging are used to achieve lead-acid battery type fast charging of the lithium ion battery, the lithium ion battery can be fully charged as much as possible, and the requirements of fast and sufficient charging of electric appliances such as electric cars are satisfied.
column8: SINO NEW ENERGY SCIENCE & TECHNOLOGY SUZHOU CO LTD

column6: Filtering charging circuit of mobile power supply
column7: The utility model relates to a filtering charging circuit of a mobile power supply. The circuit comprises a step-down rectifier circuit, an output fast charging circuit, an output filter circuit and acharging alarm circuit. The step-down rectifier circuit is coupled to the power supply and outputs a voltage signal. The output fast charging circuit is coupled to the step-down rectifier circuit toreceive the voltage signal and output a charging signal. Wherein the output filter circuit is coupled to the output fast charging circuit and charges the lithium battery after filtering a charging signal, the charging alarm circuit is coupled to the output filter circuit and is used for detecting the voltage in the lithium battery, and once the voltage in the lithium battery reaches a bearable voltage peak value, the charging alarm circuit gives an alarm prompt. People can be timely reminded to disconnect a charging circuit between the lithium battery and the power supply, overvoltage chargingof the lithium battery is reduced, and the service life of the lithium battery is prolonged.

column6: Screening and formulating method for fast charging strategy of lithium ion battery
column7: The invention relates to the technical field of batteries, and particularly provides a rapid screening and formulating method for a lithium ion battery rapid charging strategy, and the method comprises the following steps: carrying out the at least one section of large-current charging on a lithium ion three-electrode battery, and obtaining an optimal rapid charging strategy; and carrying out strategy cycle test on the two-electrode battery with the same system as the lithium ion three-electrode battery by using the optimal fast charging strategy, and verifying and/or optimizing the optimal fast charging strategy. According to the method disclosed by the invention, the optimal rapid charging strategy can be rapidly screened out in a mode of segmented rapid charging circulation of the lithium ion battery, so that the risk of lithium precipitation on the surface of the negative plate during large-current charging of the lithium ion battery is greatly reduced, the decline of the battery is slowed down, and the cycle life of the lithium ion battery is prolonged.

column6: INCREASING CYCLING LIFETIME OF FAST-CHARGING LITHIUM ION BATTERIES
column7: Methods, systems and battery modules are provided, which increase the cycling lifetime of fast charging lithium ion batteries. During the formation process, the charging currents are adjusted to optimize the cell formation, possibly according to the characteristics of the formation process itself, and discharge extents are partial and optimized as well, as is the overall structure of the formation process. During operation, voltage ranges are initially set to be narrow, and are broadened upon battery deterioration to maximize the overall lifetime. Current adjustments are applied in operation as well, with respect to the deteriorating capacity of the battery. Various formation and operation strategies are disclosed, as basis for specific optimizations.

column6: Soft package cell fast charging system
column7: The invention discloses a soft package cell fast charging system which comprises a power adapter electrically connected with a municipal power supply and a soft package lithium battery electrically connected with the power adapter, the power adapter is used for converting the municipal power supply from alternating current to direct current, a fast charging unit is arranged in the power adapter, and the soft package lithium battery is electrically connected with the power adapter. The fast charging unit is used for charging the soft package lithium battery, the fast charging unit comprises a filtering module, a rectification module, a control module, a charging current adjusting module and a charging current detection module, and the rectification module is electrically connected with the filtering module. According to the invention, the design is reasonable, the function of rapidly charging the soft package lithium battery can be realized, the charging current can be improved when the electric quantity is low to realize a rapid charging effect, the electric quantity of the soft package lithium battery can be rapidly improved, the charging current can be reduced when the electric quantity is high to realize a normal slow charging effect, overcharging is prevented, and the charging efficiency is improved. Therefore, the damage to the soft package lithium battery can be effectively reduced, and the service life of the soft package lithium battery is prolonged.

column6: Electrolyte additives for fast charging lithium ion batteries
column7: Lithium ion batteries and electrolytes therefor are provided, which include electrolyte additives having dithioester functional group(s) that stabilize the SEI (solid-electrolyte interface) at the surfaces of the anode material particles, and/or stabilize the CEI (cathode electrolyte interface) at the surfaces of the cathode material particles, and/or act as oxygen scavengers to prevent cell degradation. The electrolyte additives having dithioester functional group(s) may function as polymerization controlling and/or chain transfer agents that regulate the level of polymerization of other electrolyte components, such as VC (vinyl carbonate) and improve the formation and operation of the batteries. The lithium ion batteries may have metalloid-based anodes—including mostly Si, Ge and/or Sn as anode active material particles.

column6: Device and method for identifying primary lithium batteries
column7: The invention relates to a device and method for identifying primary lithium batteries. The device comprises a charger and is characterized in that the charger comprises a single-chip microcomputer and a battery voltage sampling circuit and a charging circuit which are connected with the single-chip microcomputer, wherein the battery voltage sampling circuit and the charging circuit are connectedwith a to-be-charge battery. The method is based on the principle that battery voltage rises fast when the primary lithium batteries are charged, reverse reaction cannot occur, and electric energy cannot be stored; the energy storage function of chargeable primary lithium batteries is lost after several charging cycles, and the voltage also rises quite fast during charging; secondary lithium batteries need longer time when being charged with same currents, and the charging features of the primary lithium batteries are greatly different from those of the secondary lithium batteries. The devicehas the advantages that the primary lithium batteries can be identified timely to stop charging, and the safety problem caused by the fact that the a secondary lithium battery charger is mistakenly used to charge the primary lithium batteries is solved.

column6: Fast charging system and charging method and aerial working device
